### Learned

- React lint correctly rejected the first effect-based Ask reset (`react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`). The safer pattern is a keyed remount boundary for prop-derived state.
- The A20 route test setup must set `BRAIN_DB_PATH` before importing auth-backed routes; `src/app/api/settings/rotate-token/route.test.setup.ts` now handles that.
- Full test count increased from 551 to 559 because A20 added auth/Ask regressions.

- Product/Ask and public/governance lanes returned go.
- Security/privacy found a remaining P1: private server-rendered pages still loaded private DB/provider data after only proxy cookie-presence auth.
- A22 expanded the fix from the named pages to the full scanned private SSR class:
  - Ask, item Ask, item detail, item repair, collections, topics, settings, settings tags/collections, capture duplicate detection, search, and Needs Upgrade now verify signed session cookies before private reads.
  - `src/proxy.ts` now verifies the signed `brain-session` cookie instead of accepting presence.

  - production audit also flagged nested `postcss` and `tar`;
  - this was treated as release-blocking because AI Brain uses `src/proxy.ts` in the auth boundary.
- Created and executed the A24 governance cycle:
  - `UX_v2/features/FEATURE_RELEASE_A24_DEPENDENCY_SECURITY_HOTFIX_PRD_V1_2026-06-16_22-50-00_IST.md`

  - `tar` updated/overridden to `7.5.16`;

### Learned

- The first successful deploy still exposed a real audit blocker. Future deployment gates should inspect the remote production `npm audit --omit=dev` result before declaring "clean".
- Next `16.2.9` still declared a nested PostCSS version that audit flagged; the project needed an override to force `postcss=8.5.14`, and build/APK validation proved compatibility.
- Local provider checks still warn when local Ollama is unavailable. Production provider proof must come from the remote Anthropic/Gemini checks.

### Deployed / Released

- Committed `0655f51` (`Complete UX v2 release candidate`).
- Deployed `0655f51`, then discovered dependency audit blocker.
- Committed `f9de485` (`Patch UX v2 dependency security advisories`).
- Deployed `f9de485` to production at `https://brain.arunp.in`.
- Production web is now deployed with Next `16.2.9` and remote production audit 0 vulnerabilities.
- No push, PR, APK signing, APK upload, APK publication, or distribution authorization was performed.

### Current remaining to-do

1. Stage and commit A24 deployment evidence/tracker docs if desired; do not stage root `RUNNING_LOG.md` unless explicitly approved.
2. Decide whether to push branch `codex/ai-brain-ux-v2-execution` or create a PR.
3. Obtain explicit APK publication authorization and a named distribution/signing target for `1.0.4/code5` or a later candidate.
4. Run full TalkBack spoken-order audit if required beyond A12 bounded launch smoke.
5. Resolve URL-share success decision; A12 native note share is proven and cleaned, but the deterministic URL fixture remains unresolved.

### Open questions / decisions needed

1. Should the branch be pushed or turned into a PR now that web production is hotfixed?
2. What is the APK distribution/signing target, and who authorizes publication?
3. Is bounded TalkBack launch smoke sufficient, or is a full spoken-order audit required?
4. Is native note share sufficient, or must URL-share success be proven separately?

### Session self-critique

- A24 dependency changes were applied immediately after the audit blocker was found, before writing the full A24 governance docs. The PRD/review/plan cycle was then created and used before final commit/redeploy; future urgent fixes should pause for the cycle first when feasible.
- The deploy script uses provider warn-only mode; a transient Ask provider timeout occurred during A24 deploy. A direct rerun passed, but this should remain visible rather than hidden.
- The overall user goal remains active because APK publication and remaining Android release decisions are still open.

Completed the A28 and A29 release-gate work. A28 restored Android tooling via the Homebrew Android command-line tools, booted AVD `Brain_API_36`, verified APK `1.0.5/code6`, and ran a real native Android URL share. ## Entry #130 - 2026-06-17 10:23 IST - Android Library card title RCA and prototype options created

### Summary

Completed a design-analysis slice after Arun reported that Android Library cards expand into very tall cards when item titles are long. The work inspected the supplied Android screenshots, compared current production Library card code against the Magic Patterns mobile source snapshot, identified root causes, and created a throwaway prototype with three candidate redesign options. No production app code was changed, no APK was rebuilt, no web deploy was made, and no repository push was performed.

### Evidence

- `UX_v2/execution/ANDROID_LIBRARY_CARD_TITLE_RCA_AND_OPTIONS_2026-06-17_10-19-11_IST.md`
- `UX_v2/execution/prototypes/android-library-card-options-2026-06-17_10-19-11_IST.html`
- Supplied screenshots:
  - `/Users/arun.prakash/Downloads/WhatsApp Unknown 2026-06-17 at 10.13.14/WhatsApp Image 2026-06-17 at 10.12.25 (1).jpeg`
  - `/Users/arun.prakash/Downloads/WhatsApp Unknown 2026-06-17 at 10.13.14/WhatsApp Image 2026-06-17 at 10.12.25.jpeg`

### Validation

- Confirmed both supplied screenshots are 738x1600 Android captures and visually show long titles wrapping almost word-by-word inside oversized Library cards.
- Inspected `src/components/library-list.tsx` and found the main contributing layout:
  - mobile row reserves width for checkbox, source icon, gaps, and a `shrink-0` enrichment pill;
  - title uses `break-words text-[18px] font-medium leading-[1.55]` with no line clamp;
  - metadata/status rows wrap below and add height.
- Inspected Magic Patterns mobile snapshot at `UX_v2/execution/ANDROID_A0_MAGIC_PATTERNS_MOBILE_SOURCE_SNAPSHOT_2026-06-16_08-32-30_IST/source/pages/MobileLibrary.tsx` and confirmed it uses compact mobile title treatment: `text-[15px] font-semibold leading-snug line-clamp-2`, selection only in select mode, and badges/meta below the title.
- Created a self-contained prototype HTML that can be opened directly in a browser and contains three alternatives:
  - Option A: compact Magic Patterns-aligned card.
  - Option B: status-first two-tier card.
  - Option C: dense inbox row.

### Release state

- Web UX v2 remains production deployed from earlier work; no new deploy happened in this slice.
- Android private sideload APK `1.0.6/code7` remains the latest local artifact from A34; no new APK build happened.
- This slice is analysis/prototype only. It intentionally did not edit `src/components/library-list.tsx`.
- Recommended implementation direction is Option A, pending Arun's prototype review.

### Current remaining to-do

1. Arun reviews `UX_v2/execution/prototypes/android-library-card-options-2026-06-17_10-19-11_IST.html` and chooses Option A, B, C, or a hybrid.
2. If Option A is approved, create the next PRD/review/implementation-plan cycle for Android Library card compaction before production code execution.
3. Implement the selected card treatment in `src/components/library-list.tsx`, with mobile-first title clamping, status hierarchy cleanup, and select-mode handling.
4. Validate on Android/mobile viewport with long YouTube title, long article title, enrichment failed, metadata-only warning, full-text item, and selected/bulk state.
5. Rebuild a fresh private sideload APK only after implementation approval and validation.

### State snapshot

- **Current phase / version:** Android Library card title issue diagnosed; prototype options ready for Arun review; no production code change yet.
- **Active branch(es):** `codex/ai-brain-ux-v2-execution` tracking `origin/codex/ai-brain-ux-v2-execution`

## Entry #134 - 2026-06-17 10:42 IST - Android Library compact card plan adversarial review completed

### Summary

Completed Arun-requested adversarial review of the Android Library compact card Option A implementation plan. The review report is a conditional no-go for execution as written. Main issues: the plan targets a shared web/Android `LibraryList` component without strong desktop blast-radius controls, keeps a contradictory source icon in the title row while adding source logos below, leaves mobile checkbox/select-mode behavior unresolved, and allows the Android-specific fix to be considered complete without mandatory Android WebView/APK evidence. No plan revision or production code change was made in this adversarial-review pass.

### Evidence

- `UX_v2/execution/ANDROID_LIBRARY_COMPACT_CARD_OPTION_A_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN_ADVERSARIAL_REVIEW_2026-06-17_10-39-34_IST.md`

  - `src/components/enriching-pill.tsx`

  - `src/app/library/page.tsx`
  - `package.json`

- Used the adversarial-review skill and report template.
- Generated timestamped review path with the adversarial-review report path helper.
- Verified report exists and is 182 lines.
- Report explicitly includes P0/P1/P2/P3 sections, revised recommendations, go/no-go recommendation, and plan revision inputs.

### Release state

- Review/report only.
- No app source code changed.
- No APK rebuild, web deploy, production data mutation, commit, or push in this slice.

### Current remaining to-do

5. Add local/provenance-safe logo asset policy and metadata overflow priority rules.

  - `src/lib/theme.ts` now resolves missing, legacy `system`, and invalid theme cookies to Light, with explicit Dark preserved only by explicit choice.
  - `src/lib/theme.test.ts` covers resolver, guards, migration, and cookie serialization.
  - `src/components/theme-bootstrap.tsx` no longer follows OS `matchMedia` and migrates legacy/invalid cookies to Light.

- Implemented Android Library compact card/source-logo behavior:
  - Added `src/components/source-logo.tsx` with local decorative source marks and no remote/CDN logo fetch.
  - Updated `src/components/item-enrichment-watch.tsx` with compact enrichment pass-through.
  - Updated `src/components/library-list.tsx` with separate mobile `md:hidden` compact card branch and preserved desktop `hidden md:flex` branch, visible mobile checkboxes, two-line title clamp, source logo plus readable source text, quality badge, compact enrichment, relative time, warning suppression for duplicate metadata-only YouTube warnings, and compact metadata cap.

  - APK SHA-256: `d56ce784240277896f344e83cecec2e5cf921279a14c87beed66a5846b33ff46`; size `7.5M`.
  - Installed on `Brain_API_36` emulator with `/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools/platform-tools/adb install -r data/artifacts/brain-debug-v1.0.7-code8.apk` -> `Success`.
  - Confirmed installed package `com.arunprakash.brain`, versionName `1.0.7`, versionCode `8`.
  - Android OS dark mode was enabled; Android WebView report `UX_v2/execution/WEB_EXPERIENCE_REVAMP_VISUAL_EVIDENCE_2026-06-15_21-48-07_IST/android-webview-compact-light-first-2026-06-17_17-28-22_IST/android-webview-runtime-report.redacted.json` has `issueCount: 0`.
  - Android WebView report proves Settings Light under OS dark/no theme cookie, explicit Dark and Light restore, mobile Library branch visible with desktop branch hidden, 34 production cards, 34 visible mobile checkboxes, compact source-logo metadata, two selected checkboxes, selected Ask route, and item-detail navigation.
- Ran and reran final quality gates:
  - `npm run lint` passed.
  - `npm run typecheck` passed.
  - `npm test` passed: 571 tests, 79 suites, 0 failures.
  - `npm run build` passed with the existing `unpdf` warning.
  - `git diff --check` passed.
  - Strict conflict-marker scan found no matches.
  - Static theme scan found no OS dark hook or old System preference copy; remaining `system` hits are data roles, tests, or non-theme references.
  - Source-logo scan found no remote/CDN/logo-fetch path in `src/components/source-logo.tsx` or `src/components/library-list.tsx`.
- Cleaned local validation residue:
  - Removed exact local QA fixture items and fixture collection/tag/topic rows from `data/brain.sqlite`; verified matching item/taxonomy counts are `0`.
  - Stopped local dev server on port `3048`.
  - Cleared temporary Android emulator app data/session with `adb shell pm clear com.arunprakash.brain`.
  - Removed WebView debug port forward and shut down the emulator.

### Cross-lane notes

- The compact-card implementation intentionally stayed in `src/components/library-list.tsx`, `src/components/item-enrichment-watch.tsx`, and new `src/components/source-logo.tsx`; protected Library page/filter/search/header/bottom-nav files were not changed.
- Light-first changed global theme behavior and Android native shell settings, so it was deployed together with the compact-card web build and validated in both production browser and Android WebView.
- The fresh private APK is a debug sideload artifact only. Public/store distribution remains a separate owner-approved release-signing/distribution lane.
- Existing unrelated dirty/untracked historical docs and Telegram plan files remain untouched and should not be swept into a broad commit.

### Learned

- The Android APK is a thin Capacitor/WebView shell to `https://brain.arunp.in`; production web deployment is the primary delivery path for online Library UI changes, while the fresh APK matters for native shell/offline fallback/versioned private sideload metadata.
- `adb` was not on PATH, but the project SDK path in `android/local.properties` pointed to `/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools`; `adb` and `emulator` were available there.
- Android WebView debug protocol was available in the debug APK. It allowed redacted DOM/route validation for private Library content without saving screenshots.
- The first Android harness attempt assumed theme controls were native `input[type=radio]`; the actual `ThemeToggle` uses `button role="radio"`. Corrected harness rerun passed with `issueCount: 0`.
- Production pre-deploy backup is on host `brain`, not the local Mac; verified via SSH at `/opt/brain/data/backups/ux-v2-compact-light-first-predeploy-2026-06-17_14-59-33_IST.sqlite`.

### Deployed / Released

- Created and verified remote production backup:
  - `/opt/brain/data/backups/ux-v2-compact-light-first-predeploy-2026-06-17_14-59-33_IST.sqlite`
  - SHA-256 `9c0afa8958178767e4918ac5f1628b1f5e39aae0ecdbe9295b65057295e62c78`
  - Size `4.5M`
- Deployed production web with `BRAIN_AI_PROVIDER_WARN_ONLY=1 bash scripts/deploy.sh`.
- Deploy completed typecheck, lint, tests, env check, build, artifact sync, remote native dependency repair, service restart, authenticated health check, remote AI provider check, and Telegram webhook reachability. Local AI provider check was warn-only because local Ollama was unavailable. Telegram live smoke remained skipped because `TELEGRAM_RELEASE=1` was not set.
- Current production status after deploy:
  - `ssh brain "sudo systemctl is-active brain"` -> `active`
  - Authenticated `/api/health` from host `brain` -> `200`
  - Public root `https://brain.arunp.in/` -> `307` to unlock, expected for unauthenticated private app access.
- Fresh private debug APK built and validated:
  - `data/artifacts/brain-debug-v1.0.7-code8.apk`
  - SHA-256 `d56ce784240277896f344e83cecec2e5cf921279a14c87beed66a5846b33ff46`
  - Installed successfully on `Brain_API_36` emulator and Android WebView QA passed with `issueCount: 0`.

### Current remaining to-do

1. None for the requested private production web + private debug APK scope of compact-card/source-logo and Light-first theme.
2. Future public/store Android distribution remains out of scope and requires a separate owner-approved PRD, adversarial review, implementation plan, signing/distribution plan, accessibility posture, and release gate.
3. If a commit/PR is requested later, stage exact owned files only and avoid sweeping unrelated dirty/untracked historical docs, Telegram docs, databases, secrets, or APK binaries unless Arun explicitly asks.

### Open questions / decisions needed

1. None for this requested scope.
2. Future decision owner: Arun must approve any public/store distribution path.

### Session self-critique

- The Android WebView validation initially looked blocked because `adb` was not on PATH; checking `android/local.properties` found the SDK path and avoided an unnecessary blocker.
- The first Android CDP harness clicked nonexistent `input[type=radio]` controls; rerunning against actual `button role="radio"` controls fixed the false failures.
- A temporary production QA session token was issued for Android WebView validation. It was not printed or saved, and emulator app data was cleared after validation.
- Production Library screenshots were intentionally avoided to prevent private-content leakage; redacted DOM/route evidence was used instead.

### Action items for the next agent

1. Treat compact-card/source-logo and Light-first private production scope as complete unless new bugs are reported.
2. Start a separate governance cycle before public/store Android distribution or release-signing work.
3. If asked to commit/push, review `git status --short` carefully, stage exact owned paths only, and leave unrelated historical/untracked docs out unless explicitly included by Arun.

## Entry #138 - 2026-06-24 20:57 IST - Recall daily sync live gates fixed and first-apply readiness prepared

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Arun's active Recall daily-sync goal to fix the blocked live call path, research Recall developer docs, validate live spikes, and prepare a safe first capped Recall -> AI Brain apply. Arun also updated the goal to say: "Always Apend when using the codex running log skill."

### Planned since last entry

Entry #137 closed compact-card/source-logo and Light-first private production release work. Arun then opened a new Recall daily-sync lane: research Recall developer docs, create PM/architecture/QA/project-management artifacts, run adversarial reviews, design and execute spikes, fix the issue where local private gates stopped before the live Recall call, and prepare an end-to-end daily import path from Recall into AI Brain.

The current turn continued that lane under the no-apply boundary. The explicit production write approval text has not been provided, so the task was to keep the proof chain fresh enough to execute safely later, harden documentation gates, and append this running-log milestone under the updated objective.

### Done

- Fixed the root blocker behind "the live call still did not run because the local private gates stopped first":
  - live gate/status and live spike runners now load the checked private Recall env file path instead of requiring manual shell sourcing first;
  - `npm run recall:live-gate:require-ready -- --manifest data/private/recall-live-spikes/controlled-samples.json` now reports `ready_for_approved_live_spikes` with private evidence and env-file loading verified without printing secrets.
- Completed live read-only Recall validation:
  - `docs/plans/spikes/SPIKE-013-recall-rest-enumeration-2026-06-24_20-03-34_IST.md` returned `CLEAR`;
  - `docs/plans/spikes/SPIKE-014-recall-content-fidelity-2026-06-24_20-03-34_IST.md` returned `PROCEED-WITH-CHANGES`;
  - accepted fidelity risk remains: "Live Recall API detail chunks are unverified; keep production import blocked by default unless explicit fidelity flags and review are used."
- Built and validated the first capped apply guard chain:
  - `npm run check:recall-first-apply-readiness` returns `PASS_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_READINESS_GATE`;
  - latest readiness check in this slice showed 3 cards seen, 3 planned imports, zero checkpoint advancement, dry-run proof freshness remaining about 67 minutes, backup proof freshness remaining about 22 minutes, backup mode `600`, and backup integrity `ok`;
  - no production write was run.
- Added and validated no-write proof refresh support:
  - `scripts/recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.sh`;
  - `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.mjs`;
  - package scripts `recall:first-apply:proof-refresh` and `smoke:recall-first-apply-proof-refresh`;
  - the wrapper runs dry-run only, refreshes the private dry-run proof and backup proof, validates both, and reruns first-apply readiness without passing `--apply`.
- Hardened approval consistency:
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires the proof-refresh wrapper, proof-refresh report, and proof-refresh package scripts as first-class approval-chain artifacts;
  - `npm run check:recall-approval-packet` passed with 8 checked docs and 27 required scripts.
- Revalidated privacy and report gates:
  - `npm run check:recall-public-docs-privacy` passed and scanned 20 curated public Recall docs;
  - accepted SPIKE-013/SPIKE-014 report gate passed with manifest-aware privacy scanning;
  - lightweight no-apply gate pass confirmed the live gate is ready and the public docs have no obvious secret-shaped leaks.
- Created/updated no-secret Recall planning and evidence artifacts under `docs/plans/recall-sync/`, including:
  - `RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_PROOF_REFRESH_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_20-47-10_IST.md`;
  - `RECALL_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_APPROVAL_PACKET_2026-06-24_19-28-07_IST.md`;
  - `RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_READINESS_FRESHNESS_COUNTDOWN_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_20-34-12_IST.md`;
  - `RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md`;
  - `RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md`;
  - `RECALL_PUBLIC_DOCS_PRIVACY_SCAN_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_18-28-55_IST.md`.

### Cross-lane notes

- This Recall work is on `codex/ai-brain-ux-v2-execution`.
- Do not sweep unrelated dirty/untracked historical docs, Telegram docs, databases, secrets, or APK artifacts into commits.
- Private Recall evidence remains under ignored `data/private/recall-live-spikes/`; do not print private card IDs, private titles, private source URLs, raw chunks, or the Recall API key.
- The pasted Recall API key should be rotated by Arun because it appeared in chat, even though it is not printed in public docs.

### Learned

- The original live-call failure was a local gate sequencing problem, not evidence that Recall REST was unreachable.
- Recall detail chunks are still treated as unverified, and metadata-only content remains a special fidelity class. Import is therefore intentionally blocked by default unless explicit fidelity flags and reviewed dry-run proof are present.
- Proof freshness matters operationally: the current backup proof was still valid in the latest check, but it will expire before long. Use the no-write proof refresh wrapper rather than weakening the freshness gate.
- `check-recall-live-spike-reports` requires explicit `--enumeration` and `--fidelity` paths; manifest privacy scanning is implicit when `--manifest` is provided.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, or released this session. No Recall production apply, production deploy, scheduler enablement, or checkpoint advancement was performed.

### Documents created or updated this period

**Created:**
- `scripts/recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.sh` - no-write proof-refresh wrapper for stale/near-expiry first-apply dry-run and backup proof.
- `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.mjs` - fixture smoke proving the proof-refresh wrapper refuses without confirmation and refreshes proof without apply.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_PROOF_REFRESH_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_20-47-10_IST.md` - no-secret execution report for the proof-refresh wrapper.

**Updated:**
- `package.json` - added Recall first-apply proof-refresh package scripts and related Recall gate scripts.
- `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` - requires proof-refresh artifacts and scripts in the approval-chain consistency gate.
- `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs` - readiness now reports proof freshness countdown and enforces the minimum freshness floor.
- `scripts/recall-first-capped-apply.sh` - guarded first capped apply wrapper reruns readiness and honors the freshness floor before any apply.
- `scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs` - curated current-doc privacy scan includes the latest Recall approval/evidence docs.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_APPROVAL_PACKET_2026-06-24_19-28-07_IST.md` - documents the guarded wrapper and no-write refresh path.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` - tracks RDS-026b8 and the remaining first capped apply gate.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` - records current Recall completion status and remaining production gates.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_PUBLIC_DOCS_PRIVACY_SCAN_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_18-28-55_IST.md` - documents the 20-file current public-doc privacy scan.
- `RUNNING_LOG.md` - this append-only entry.

### Current remaining to-do

1. If Arun wants the first capped write, use the exact approval text in `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_APPROVAL_PACKET_2026-06-24_19-28-07_IST.md`.
2. Immediately before any apply, rerun `npm run check:recall-first-apply-readiness`.
3. If dry-run or backup proof is stale or below `minFreshnessRemainingMinutes`, run `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_REFRESH_CONFIRM=1 npm run recall:first-apply:proof-refresh`, then rerun readiness.
4. Run the first capped write only through `BRAIN_RECALL_SYNC_ENABLED=1 BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_APPROVAL="<exact text>" npm run recall:first-capped-apply`.
5. After apply, run the post-apply report gate against `data/private/recall-live-spikes/first-apply-report.json` before any deploy or scheduler decision.
6. Keep production deploy and scheduler enablement blocked until the first apply validates cleanly and Arun separately approves those steps.

### Open questions / decisions needed

1. Arun must explicitly decide whether to approve the first capped Recall -> AI Brain write.
2. Arun should rotate the Recall API key because it was pasted into chat earlier.
3. After a clean manual first apply, Arun must separately decide whether daily scheduler enablement is acceptable.

### Session self-critique

- This entry records a strong no-apply readiness state, not production completion.
- The private backup proof was still valid at the latest check but has limited time remaining; future agents should not assume it remains fresh.
- The worktree contains many untracked Recall docs because the broader lane has not been staged or committed; future commit work must stage exact owned files only.
- Running-log append happened under Arun's updated direct objective instruction to always append when using the codex running log skill.

### Action items for the next agent

1. Start with `npm run check:recall-first-apply-readiness`.
2. If readiness fails only because proof is stale/near-expiry, run `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_REFRESH_CONFIRM=1 npm run recall:first-apply:proof-refresh`; do not run apply as part of refresh.
3. If readiness passes and Arun provides the exact approval text, run `npm run recall:first-capped-apply` with `BRAIN_RECALL_SYNC_ENABLED=1` and `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_APPROVAL` set exactly.
4. Run `npm run check:recall-apply-report` on the private first apply report before any deploy/scheduler work.
5. Keep private Recall values private and avoid printing card-level evidence.

## Entry #140 - 2026-06-24 21:11 IST - Recall first-apply private proof refreshed

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Active Recall daily-sync goal continuation after Entry #139, with the first capped apply private backup proof nearing expiry.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #139 added the no-write ready-or-refresh wrapper and left the first capped apply approval as the next production gate. The immediate operational risk was that private dry-run/backup proof could expire while waiting for explicit write approval.

### Done

- Ran `npm run recall:first-apply:ready-or-refresh` without refresh confirmation. It returned `ready_without_refresh`, but the backup proof had less than 10 minutes of freshness remaining.
- Ran the same wrapper with a 15-minute maintenance freshness floor and explicit refresh confirmation:
  - `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_READY_REFRESH_CONFIRM=1`
  - `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_MIN_FRESHNESS_REMAINING_MINUTES=15`
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:ready-or-refresh`
- The wrapper detected `proof_expiring_soon` for the backup proof, delegated to the no-write proof-refresh wrapper, and refreshed:
  - private dry-run proof;
  - private SQLite backup proof.
- Post-refresh aggregate readiness:
  - `PASS_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_READINESS_GATE`;
  - dry-run proof freshness about 120 minutes at refresh time;
  - backup proof freshness about 120 minutes at refresh time;
  - cards seen: 3;
  - cards planned for import: 3;
  - checkpoint advanced: false;
  - backup integrity: `ok`;
  - backup mode: `600`.
- Created no-secret execution report:
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_PROOF_REFRESH_ACTUAL_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_21-11-03_IST.md`
- Updated the curated public-doc privacy scan to include the actual proof refresh report.
- Validation passed after refresh:
  - `npm run check:recall-first-apply-readiness`;
  - `npm run check:recall-approval-packet`;
  - `npm run check:recall-public-docs-privacy` scanned 22 curated docs;
  - accepted SPIKE-013/SPIKE-014 report gate;
  - `git diff --check` on touched Recall files.

### Cross-lane notes

- No production apply, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.
- The proof refresh made read-only Recall API calls and wrote private proof files under ignored `data/private/recall-live-spikes/`.
- Public report/log content remains aggregate-only.

### Learned

- The 15-minute maintenance floor is useful for avoiding an approval-time race while preserving the default 5-minute apply gate.
- Ready-or-refresh correctly distinguishes proof-maintenance failures from non-refreshable gate failures.

### Deployed / Released

- Added fixture smoke coverage:
  - `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-ready-or-refresh.mjs`
  - package script `npm run smoke:recall-first-apply-ready-or-refresh`
- Wrapper behavior:
  - runs `check-recall-first-apply-readiness` first;
  - exits with `ready_without_refresh` when proof is already fresh;
  - refreshes only for proof freshness/existence failures such as `proof_expiring_soon`, `stale_report`, `missing_report`, `future_dated_report`, `stale_backup`, `missing_backup`, or `future_dated_backup`;
  - requires `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_READY_REFRESH_CONFIRM=1` or `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_REFRESH_CONFIRM=1` before refresh;
  - delegates refresh to `scripts/recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.sh`;
  - stops on non-refreshable gates such as live report proof, approval packet, privacy, policy, fidelity, or cap failures;
  - never passes `--apply`.
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_READY_OR_REFRESH_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_21-02-06_IST.md`.
- Updated approval and evidence docs/checkers:
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires ready-or-refresh docs and package scripts;
  - `scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs` now scans 21 curated Recall public docs;
  - approval packet, current-state audit, project tracker, and privacy scan report now document the ready-or-refresh wrapper.
- Validation passed:
  - `bash -n scripts/recall-first-apply-ready-or-refresh.sh`
  - `node --check scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-ready-or-refresh.mjs`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-first-apply-ready-or-refresh`
  - `npm run check:recall-approval-packet`
  - `npm run check:recall-public-docs-privacy`
  - `npm run check:recall-first-apply-readiness`

### Learned

- The approval consistency gate is useful as a drift detector: after adding ready-or-refresh, it caught two stale doc snippets before readiness could pass again.
- The backup proof was still above the 5-minute freshness floor during validation, but future agents should expect it to expire soon and use ready-or-refresh before any approval-window apply.

## Entry #146 - 2026-06-24 22:51 IST - First-apply key rotation evidence gate validated

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued Recall sync goal work after Entry #145; the first-write path still needed a machine-verifiable local evidence gate so the wrapper cannot run a live apply with a Recall API key file that predates the chat-exposure rotation checkpoint.

Entry #145 added the exact `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ACK` gate and left first capped apply blocked until Arun rotates the Recall API key, provides the exact approval text, and provides the exact key rotation acknowledgement. This session tightened that blocker so the wrapper also checks local private env-file metadata before any live Recall call or database write.

### Done

- Added a local key rotation evidence gate:
  - `scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` checks only safe metadata for `data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env`: exists, private path, git ignored, not tracked, owner-only permissions, and mtime after `2026-06-24T15:54:17.000Z`.
  - The checker never reads or prints the Recall API key.
  - `scripts/smoke-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` proves stale metadata fails, fresh metadata passes, and failure output does not print env-file contents.
- Wired the gate into first capped apply:
  - `scripts/recall-first-capped-apply.sh` now runs the evidence gate for real non-fixture applies before readiness, live Recall calls, or DB writes.
  - `scripts/smoke-recall-first-capped-apply.mjs` now proves stale local key rotation evidence stops the wrapper before readiness/apply and before an apply report can be created.
- Added package commands:
  - `npm run check:recall-key-rotation-evidence`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-key-rotation-evidence`
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_GATE_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_21-33-19_IST.md`.
- Updated first-apply governance docs and gates:
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_APPROVAL_PACKET_2026-06-24_19-28-07_IST.md` now requires the evidence gate before first capped apply.
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires the evidence report, package scripts, approval-packet snippets, audit snippets, and tracker snippets.
  - `scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs` and `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_PUBLIC_DOCS_PRIVACY_SCAN_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_18-28-55_IST.md` now include 24 curated current docs, including the new evidence report.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` and `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` now record the local evidence gate, `RDS-026b12`, and the current real blocker.
  - `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs` now names all remaining first-write gates in its next step: explicit Arun approval, key rotation acknowledgement, and local key rotation evidence.
- Validation passed:
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/smoke-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs`
  - `bash -n scripts/recall-first-capped-apply.sh`
  - `node --check scripts/smoke-recall-first-capped-apply.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-key-rotation-evidence`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-first-capped-apply`
  - `npm run check:recall-approval-packet`
  - `npm run check:recall-public-docs-privacy`
  - `npm run check:recall-first-apply-readiness`
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:ready-or-refresh`
- Real local evidence gate behavior was verified:
  - `npm run check:recall-key-rotation-evidence` currently fails closed with `env_file_not_rotated_after_checkpoint`.
  - This is expected because the current ignored private Recall env file mtime predates the key-rotation checkpoint.
  - No key material, private Recall card IDs, private titles, source URLs, raw chunks, card content, apply report payloads, or database rows were printed.

### Cross-lane notes

- No production apply, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.
- The gate order is now: exact first-write approval, `BRAIN_RECALL_SYNC_ENABLED=1`, exact key rotation acknowledgement, local key rotation evidence, no-write readiness, live/API/apply path, post-apply report validation.
- Private proof and Recall data remain under ignored private paths; keep them out of public docs and final summaries.

### Learned

- The previous local private gates were safe but operator-dependent. A metadata gate makes the key-rotation requirement machine-checkable without reading or exposing the key.
- Machine readiness can pass while security evidence intentionally fails. Future agents should treat `PASS_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_READINESS_GATE` as necessary but not sufficient for first capped apply.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, or checkpointed this session.

5. After first capped apply, run the post-apply report review before any deploy or scheduler decision.

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued Recall sync goal work after Entry #147; the first-write and proof-refresh paths respected key rotation evidence, but the future scheduled wrapper still needed the same protection before any enabled timer could run live Recall work.

Entry #147 left the system blocked on stale local key evidence and correctly made `ready-or-refresh` stop before proof refresh. This session checked the future scheduler lane and found that `scripts/recall-scheduled-apply.sh` required scheduler enable flags, live API confirmation, live-spike proof, dry-run proof, backup proof, and post-apply review, but did not yet require key rotation evidence before scheduled live work.

### Done

- Added system-env-file support to `scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs`:
  - local first-apply mode still requires the ignored private Recall env file under `data/private/recall-live-spikes/`, ignored, untracked, owner-only, and newer than the rotation checkpoint;
  - `--system-env-file` skips git worktree checks for production-style env files and requires restrictive file permissions plus mtime after the rotation checkpoint;
  - the checker still reads only file metadata and never reads or prints the Recall API key.
- Updated `scripts/recall-scheduled-apply.sh`:
  - real non-fixture scheduled live mode now runs key rotation evidence before live-spike proof, report directory creation, dry-run, backup, apply, or report validation;
  - default production evidence file is `${BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE:-/etc/brain/.env}`.

  - proves confirmed live scheduled mode with stale key evidence fails before report directory creation;

- Updated deploy and static gates:

  - `scripts/check-recall-scheduler-artifacts.mjs` now requires the wrapper key-evidence call, system env-file mode, deploy copy, and scheduled smoke stale-key coverage.

## Entry #150 - 2026-06-24 23:29 IST - First-apply readiness now surfaces key evidence directly

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued Recall sync goal work after Entry #149; the real live/proof-refresh path was still stopped by stale local key evidence, and the single readiness command needed to show that blocker directly without bypassing the key-rotation requirement.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #149 left the next milestone blocked on key rotation evidence and stale first-apply proof. The deploy override path was hardened, but `npm run check:recall-first-apply-readiness` still surfaced stale proof without making local key rotation evidence a first-class checked gate. Direct `npm run recall:first-apply:proof-refresh` also needed the same pre-live key-evidence guard if someone called it directly instead of via ready-or-refresh.

### Done

- Consolidated local key evidence into first-apply readiness:
  - `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs` now runs `scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` by default and includes a `key_rotation_evidence` checked item in its JSON output.
  - It accepts `--key-rotated-after` and `--skip-key-rotation-evidence` so real paths can enforce the rotation checkpoint while fixture smokes stay synthetic.
  - The real readiness command now reports both current blockers in one place: stale key evidence and stale dry-run/backup proof.
- Hardened proof-maintenance wrappers:
  - `scripts/recall-first-apply-ready-or-refresh.sh` now passes the key-rotation checkpoint through to readiness and still prechecks key evidence before any readiness or refresh work on the real path.
  - `scripts/recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.sh` now checks local key rotation evidence before any non-fixture live Recall dry-run refresh, so direct proof refresh stops before live Recall work while the exposed-key rotation evidence is stale.
  - `scripts/recall-first-capped-apply.sh` now passes the key-rotation checkpoint through readiness and keeps fixture smoke paths isolated with the explicit smoke-only skip.
- Expanded smoke coverage:
  - `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs` now proves stale key evidence fails readiness without printing env-file contents.
  - `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.mjs` now proves direct proof refresh with confirmation stops on stale key evidence before creating dry-run or backup proof.
  - Existing ready-or-refresh and first-capped-apply smokes continue to pass with the new readiness gate.
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_READINESS_KEY_EVIDENCE_CONSOLIDATION_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_23-23-20_IST.md`.
- Updated governance and docs:

  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_PUBLIC_DOCS_PRIVACY_SCAN_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_18-28-55_IST.md` records the 27-file scope and the readiness key-evidence consolidation report.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` records `RDS-026b14`, the new artifact, and the updated dependency for first capped apply.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` records that readiness and direct proof refresh now surface/enforce key evidence.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_APPROVAL_PACKET_2026-06-24_19-28-07_IST.md` and `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SNAPSHOT_IMPORT_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK_2026-06-24_10-47-45_IST.md` now tell operators that proof refresh only runs after key evidence passes.
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now enforces the new report and updated audit, tracker, runbook, approval packet, and privacy-scan references.
- Validation passed:
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs`

### Cross-lane notes

### Learned

- A gate can be technically enforced by wrappers but still operationally confusing if the main readiness command does not show it. Moving key evidence into readiness makes the next action unambiguous.
- Direct lower-level maintenance commands need the same stale-key protection as orchestration wrappers; otherwise a well-meaning operator could bypass the safer ready-or-refresh path.

## Entry #149 - 2026-06-24 23:17 IST - Deploy override path now requires remote key rotation evidence

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued Recall sync goal work after Entry #148; the scheduled wrapper had key rotation evidence protection, but production deploy override windows still needed the same remote evidence check before allowing disabled preflight bypasses.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #148 left first apply and scheduled live mode blocked on stale key evidence and stale proof. This session inspected the deploy path and found that `scripts/deploy.sh` allowed `BRAIN_RECALL_ALLOW_ENABLED_FLAGS=1` and `BRAIN_RECALL_ALLOW_EXISTING_TIMER=1` override windows without first requiring remote key rotation evidence for the production env file.

### Done

- Added `remote_recall_key_rotation_evidence_preflight()` to `scripts/deploy.sh`:
  - only runs when Recall deploy override flags are used;
  - checks `${BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE:-/etc/brain/.env}` on the remote host by metadata only;
  - requires the evidence file to exist, be owner-readable, reject group/other permissions, and have an mtime at or after `${BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATED_AFTER_ISO:-2026-06-24T15:54:17.000Z}`;
  - fails with a clear stale-evidence message before deploy override execution if the remote env evidence predates the checkpoint;
  - does not read or print the Recall API key.
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-scheduler-artifacts.mjs` so the static scheduler/deploy gate now requires:
  - `remote_recall_key_rotation_evidence_preflight`;
  - `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE`;
  - `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATED_AFTER_ISO`;
  - stale remote evidence failure text;
  - the deploy override message that remote Recall key rotation evidence must pass when Recall deploy overrides are used.
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DEPLOY_OVERRIDE_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_GATE_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_23-12-24_IST.md` with no-secret evidence for the new deploy override gate.
- Updated public governance docs and checkers:
  - `scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs` now scans 26 curated current Recall docs;
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_PUBLIC_DOCS_PRIVACY_SCAN_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_18-28-55_IST.md` records the 26-file scope and deploy override evidence gate;
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` records `RDS-026av5`, the new artifact, and the deploy override evidence report;
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SNAPSHOT_IMPORT_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK_2026-06-24_10-47-45_IST.md` now states that deploy override paths require remote key evidence;
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` now records the deploy override key-evidence safeguard;
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now enforces the new report and related runbook, audit, tracker, and privacy-scan references.
- Validation passed:
  - `bash -n scripts/deploy.sh`
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-scheduler-artifacts.mjs`
  - `npm run check:recall-scheduler`
  - `npm run check:recall-approval-packet`
  - `npm run check:recall-public-docs-privacy`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-scheduler-wrapper`
  - `git diff --check` on the touched deploy, checker, Recall docs, and `RUNNING_LOG.md` set before this append.

### Cross-lane notes

- No production apply, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.
- This entry only strengthens the deploy override guardrail. It does not unblock first apply because local key evidence and proof freshness are still failing closed.
- Private Recall values remained private; no API key material, private card IDs, private titles, private source URLs, raw chunks, apply report payloads, or database rows were printed.

### Learned

- The deploy override path was a separate approval surface from the scheduled wrapper. Even though it does not itself run the daily Recall apply, it can bypass disabled-service/timer preflights, so it needs remote key rotation evidence before those bypasses are accepted.
- The safest deploy-side check is metadata-only: file existence, permissions, ownership-readable mode, and modification time after the rotation checkpoint.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, or checkpointed this session.

Entry #153 said to treat the ordered status helper as the first command, avoid proof refresh/apply/deploy/scheduler while status is `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`, and refresh stale proof only after key evidence passes. ## Entry #157 - 2026-06-25 00:36 IST - Private key-rotation evidence recorder added for stale env-mtime cases

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after Entry #156 proved live Recall auth/connectivity but first-write gates still stopped on local key-rotation evidence.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #156 left the real first-apply path at `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`: live auth was proven with a read-only future-window `/cards` call, but first proof refresh/apply still needed key rotation outside chat plus local evidence. This session fixed the brittle mtime-only evidence workflow without weakening write gates: add a no-secret private evidence recorder that can prove post-rotation auth via the read-only live auth probe and write an ignored owner-only metadata file for the key-evidence gate.

### Done

- Extended `scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs`.
  - The gate still accepts a private Recall env file whose mtime is after the rotation checkpoint.
  - It now can also accept fresh ignored, untracked, owner-only private evidence at `data/private/recall-live-spikes/key-rotation-evidence.json`.
  - Private evidence must match the env file path and rotation checkpoint, be recorded after the checkpoint, include `ackPhraseAccepted: true`, and include a successful read-only live auth probe with HTTP 200.
  - Stale private evidence still fails; stale env mtime plus missing evidence still fails.
- Added `scripts/record-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs`.
  - Requires exact `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ACK` text.
  - Runs `scripts/run-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs` with the ignored private env file and a future date window.
  - Writes only metadata to `data/private/recall-live-spikes/key-rotation-evidence.json`.
  - Does not apply, deploy, enable scheduler, advance checkpoint, write AI Brain DB rows, fetch card detail, or store key material/private Recall content.
- Added `scripts/smoke-recall-key-rotation-evidence-record.mjs`.
  - Proves the recorder refuses without exact acknowledgement.
  - Proves the recorder runs a read-only auth probe against a mock server.
  - Proves the private evidence file is owner-only and does not contain key material.
  - Proves stale env mtime can pass only when fresh private evidence exists.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` with fresh/stale private evidence cases.
- Added package scripts:

  - `npm run smoke:recall-key-rotation-evidence-record`
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.

## Entry #156 - 2026-06-25 00:19 IST - Standalone live auth probe proves Recall reachability without first-apply gates

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after the user-highlighted issue that the live call still did not run because local private gates stopped first.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #155 left first capped apply and proof refresh correctly blocked by stale key-rotation evidence. The current session fixed the diagnostic gap without weakening write safety: add a standalone no-write, read-only live Recall auth probe so live API reachability can be proven independently of first-apply readiness, stale proof, backup proof, or key-rotation evidence gates.

### Done

- Checked the current Recall developer API docs again.
  - Current API is read-only.
  - Base URL is `https://backend.getrecall.ai/api/v1`.
  - Auth uses bearer API keys.
  - `GET /api/v1/cards` supports date filters.
- Added `scripts/run-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs`.
  - It loads the ignored private env file only after file-safety checks.
  - It requires `--confirm-live-api` or `BRAIN_RECALL_CONFIRM_LIVE_API=1`.
  - It makes exactly one read-only `GET /cards` call using a future date window.
  - It prints only status/count metadata.
  - It does not fetch card details, print card IDs/titles/source URLs/chunks/raw bodies, read/write the AI Brain DB, create proof, apply, deploy, enable a scheduler, or advance a checkpoint.
  - It explicitly says passing the probe does not satisfy first-apply key-rotation evidence or write approval gates.
- Added `scripts/smoke-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs`.
  - Local mock-server smoke covers missing confirmation, missing key, success, bearer auth, default future date filters, no private output, and `401` auth failure mapping without key leakage.
- Added package scripts:
  - `npm run recall:live-auth-probe`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-live-auth-probe`
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_LIVE_AUTH_PROBE_LOCAL_GATE_FIX_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_00-11-33_IST.md`.
- Updated the public-doc privacy scan scope to 31 curated files and added the new report.
- Updated the project tracker and completion audit with the new milestone, task `RDS-026b18`, and the distinction between live connectivity and first-write readiness.
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` so the approval consistency gate now requires the new scripts, report, tracker task, and 31-file public-doc scan language.
- Real live probe result:
  - `npm run recall:live-auth-probe -- --env-file data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env --confirm-live-api`
  - returned `ok: true`, `httpStatus: 200`, `authenticated: true`, `reachable: true`, `totalCount: 0`, `resultCount: 0`.
  - No private Recall content or secret value was printed.
- Validation passed:
  - `node --check scripts/run-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/smoke-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs`
  - `node --check scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-live-auth-probe`
  - `npm run check:recall-public-docs-privacy` with `scannedFiles: 31`
  - `npm run check:recall-approval-packet`
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:status`
- Real first-apply status remains:
  - `status: blocked_key_rotation_evidence`;
  - approval packet, live-spike proof, live-gate status, and public-doc privacy are green;
  - dry-run and backup proof are stale behind the key-evidence blocker.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #156 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- The new live auth probe is diagnostic only. It must not be treated as apply readiness, proof freshness, key rotation evidence, post-apply review, deploy approval, or scheduler approval.
- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.
- Private Recall values remained private; no API key material, private card IDs, private titles, private source URLs, raw chunks, proof payloads, apply payloads, backup payloads, or database rows were printed.

### Learned

- The phrase "live call did not run" had two meanings. Recall API auth/connectivity can now be tested separately and is proven reachable; first-write proof refresh/apply is still intentionally blocked by key-rotation evidence.
- A future-window `GET /cards` probe is the smallest useful live check because it exercises bearer auth and the documented list endpoint while avoiding private card detail fetches and returning zero results in the current account state.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, or checkpointed this session.

## Entry #159 - 2026-06-25 00:59 IST - Post-rotation prepare wrapper becomes current first-apply pickup path

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after the user resumed from pause; focus was reducing the remaining local gate friction before the first capped apply without weakening key-rotation/write safety.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #158 corrected append order and said to treat Entry #157 as the detailed private key-rotation evidence recorder milestone. It left first capped apply blocked until the Recall API key is rotated outside chat, local key-rotation evidence passes or private evidence is recorded, stale dry-run/backup proof is refreshed, and Arun provides exact first-write approval plus the exact key-rotation acknowledgement.

This session tightened that handoff by adding a single no-write post-rotation preparation command. The command is intentionally still blocked until actual key rotation happens outside chat; it only reduces the safe operator sequence after that external action is complete.

### Done

- Added `scripts/prepare-recall-first-apply-after-rotation.mjs`.
  - Requires the exact `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ACK` before doing anything beyond status inspection.
  - Requires `BRAIN_RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_PREPARE_CONFIRM=1` before recording private evidence or refreshing stale proof.
  - Runs the ordered first-apply status helper first.
  - If key evidence is blocked only by stale/missing private evidence, it runs the private evidence recorder through the read-only live auth probe.
  - If status then becomes `needs_no_write_proof_refresh`, it delegates to the existing no-write ready-or-refresh wrapper.
  - It never runs production apply, deploy, scheduler enablement, or checkpoint advancement.
- Added `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-prepare-after-rotation.mjs`.
  - Proves missing acknowledgement fails before evidence creation.
  - Proves stale env mtime plus exact acknowledgement can record private evidence through a read-only auth probe.
  - Proves fresh private evidence can make status ready when proof is fresh.
  - Proves stale proof is refreshed through the existing no-write ready-or-refresh wrapper.
  - Proves temp private files are cleaned up and no key material is printed.
- Updated package scripts:
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:prepare-after-rotation`
  - `npm run smoke:recall-first-apply-prepare-after-rotation`
- Threaded `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE` through the first-apply readiness/status checks and first-apply shell wrappers so custom private evidence files work consistently.
- Fixed `scripts/record-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` to clear ambient `RECALL_API_KEY` before invoking the env-file live auth probe. This ensures the private evidence file proves the ignored env-file credential path instead of an unrelated shell environment key.
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` so its failure guidance now prefers the post-rotation prepare wrapper after real key rotation, while keeping the recorder as the lower-level evidence command.
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_POST_ROTATION_PREPARE_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_00-50-28_IST.md`.
- Updated the public docs privacy scan scope to 33 curated files and refreshed `RECALL_PUBLIC_DOCS_PRIVACY_SCAN_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-24_18-28-55_IST.md`.
- Updated the project tracker with artifact coverage and task `RDS-026b20`; `RDS-026c` now depends on the prepare wrapper returning ready before first capped apply.
- Updated the current-state completion audit, first capped apply approval packet, production runbook, and first-apply status helper report so the preferred post-rotation sequence is now the prepare wrapper, not a manual recorder-plus-refresh sequence.
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` so the approval consistency gate requires the new wrapper scripts, report, tracker task, package scripts, runbook/approval packet language, and 40 required package scripts.
- Real local first-apply state remains safely blocked:
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:status` reports `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`.
  - `data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env` is ignored, untracked, owner-only, and under the right private path, but its mtime predates the key-rotation checkpoint.
  - `data/private/recall-live-spikes/key-rotation-evidence.json` is absent.
  - dry-run and backup proof are stale behind the key-evidence blocker.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #159 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- Do not run the prepare wrapper until the Recall API key has actually been rotated outside chat and stored only in `data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env`.
- No production apply, real proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.
- Private Recall values remained private; no API key material, private card IDs, private titles, private source URLs, raw chunks, proof payloads, apply payloads, backup payloads, or database rows were printed.

### Learned

- The first-apply lane now has three distinct no-write layers:
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:status` for read-only local ordering and next commands;
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:prepare-after-rotation` for the preferred one-command post-rotation preparation after exact acknowledgement and prepare confirmation;
  - `npm run recall:first-capped-apply` for the eventual guarded write after exact approval, exact acknowledgement, fresh proof, and passing readiness.
- Clearing ambient `RECALL_API_KEY` in the recorder child process matters. Without that, a private evidence file could accidentally prove the shell environment credential path instead of the ignored env file path the gate is meant to protect.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, or checkpointed this session.

## Entry #164 - 2026-06-25 01:48 IST - Scheduled/deploy key-rotation env-file alias aligned with core apply

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after Entry #163 and investigating why local private gates can still stop real scheduled/deploy paths before live work when operators use the core apply env-file variable name.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #163 passed key-rotation evidence into the future scheduled core apply CLI. The next inspection found a naming drift: core apply supports `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ENV_FILE`, but scheduled/deploy key-evidence paths still primarily used `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE` as the env-file path. The plan was to remove that operator trap without bypassing key-rotation evidence, proof freshness, write approval, deploy approval, or scheduler approval.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/recall-scheduled-apply.sh`.
  - Added `key_rotation_env_file="${BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ENV_FILE:-${BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE:-/etc/brain/.env}}"`.
  - The scheduled wrapper-level key-evidence preflight now uses that resolved path.
  - The scheduled core apply flags now pass the same resolved path to `--key-rotation-env-file`.
  - The legacy `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE` fallback remains supported for existing runbooks/environments.
- Updated `scripts/deploy.sh`.
  - Remote key-rotation preflight now prefers `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ENV_FILE`.
  - It still falls back to `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE` and then `/etc/brain/.env`.
  - The remote shell receives both variable names set to the resolved env-file path, so older remote logic remains compatible.
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-scheduler-artifacts.mjs`.
  - Static release gate now requires both the preferred env-file variable and the legacy fallback in scheduled/deploy paths.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-scheduled-wrapper.mjs`.
  - The stale key-evidence negative case now uses `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ENV_FILE`, proving the preferred variable fails closed before report directory creation or scheduled work.
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ENV_FILE_ALIAS_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_01-44-29_IST.md`.
- Updated current docs/checkers:
  - `scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs` now scans 36 curated Recall docs;
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now checks the alias report and `RDS-026av7`;
  - production runbook now names `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ENV_FILE` as the preferred scheduled/deploy env-file path;
  - current-state audit and project tracker now record the alias fix.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #164 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- This was no-write scheduled/deploy operability hardening only.
- No live Recall API call, production dry-run, proof refresh, apply, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.
- The repo remains heavily dirty with many pre-existing unrelated app/UX changes and untracked Recall docs/scripts. Continue using targeted diffs and do not clean/reset the worktree.

### Learned

- The real first-apply status remains `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`: `data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env` is still older than the rotation checkpoint, and `data/private/recall-live-spikes/key-rotation-evidence.json` is still absent.
- The alias fix does not change that blocker. It prevents a future production/scheduler operator from setting the clearer core variable name and still having the wrapper/deploy preflight look somewhere else.
- The scheduled wrapper smoke now proves this exact alias path, not just static source text.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

- Future agents should continue to avoid broad cleanup in the dirty worktree and should not treat passing smokes as approval to run the first capped apply.

## Entry #162 - 2026-06-25 01:28 IST - Core apply CLI now has a key-rotation evidence gate

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after pause and Entry #161; Codex targeted the remaining lower-level apply bypass where someone could run `scripts/sync-recall.ts --apply` directly instead of the guarded first capped apply wrapper.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #161 made the first-apply status helper point to the approval packet and guarded wrapper. The next safe offline gap was defense in depth: the guarded wrapper checked local key-rotation evidence, but the core apply CLI did not have its own optional key-evidence proof gate. This session closed that lower-level path without running production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, or checkpoint advancement.

### Done

- Added a core apply key-rotation evidence gate to `scripts/sync-recall.ts`.
  - New apply flags: `--require-key-rotation-evidence`, `--key-rotation-env-file`, `--key-rotation-evidence-file`, `--no-key-rotation-evidence-file`, `--key-rotated-after`, and `--key-rotation-system-env-file`.
  - New env controls: `BRAIN_RECALL_REQUIRE_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE=1`, `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_ENV_FILE`, `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_FILE`, `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATED_AFTER_ISO`, and `BRAIN_RECALL_KEY_ROTATION_SYSTEM_ENV_FILE=1`.
  - The gate calls `scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` before live-spike proof, dry-run proof, backup proof, live Recall API use, importer writes, or checkpoint advancement.
- Wired `scripts/recall-first-capped-apply.sh` through the new core gate on the real non-fixture path.
  - The wrapper still checks key evidence before readiness.
  - It now also passes key-evidence flags into `scripts/sync-recall.ts --apply`.
  - Fixed a `set -u` empty-array issue by using guarded expansion for the optional key-evidence argument list.
- Hardened packaged CLI smoke coverage in `scripts/smoke-recall-cli-bundle.mjs`.
  - The smoke now copies `check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` beside the bundled CLI.
  - It proves missing key evidence fails before apply.
  - It proves fresh safe metadata passes in the packaged apply path.
- Hardened wrapper smoke coverage in `scripts/smoke-recall-first-capped-apply.mjs`.
  - The smoke now statically asserts the real wrapper branch passes key-evidence flags into the core CLI.
- Updated no-secret evidence docs:
  - created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_CORE_APPLY_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_GATE_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_01-18-04_IST.md`;
  - updated the first capped apply approval packet so the lower-level debug command is key-evidence-gated;
  - updated the production runbook direct apply command with the same key-evidence flags;
  - refreshed the first capped apply wrapper report contract;
  - added tracker row `RDS-026b23`;
  - updated the current-state audit and curated public-doc privacy scan to 34 files;
  - updated `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` to directly check the new execution report.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #162 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- This was no-write safety hardening only.
- No production apply, real proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.
- The worktree remains heavily dirty from this Recall lane plus unrelated pre-existing app/UX changes; future agents should keep using targeted diffs and avoid broad cleanup.

### Learned

- Node 22 treats `--env-file` as a runtime option even when it appears after a script path if the referenced env file is missing. `scripts/sync-recall.ts` now invokes child proof checkers through `node -- <checker> ...` so checker-level `--env-file` arguments remain script arguments.
- Bash `set -u` can treat an empty array expansion as unbound in the fixture path. The first capped apply wrapper now uses the guarded optional-array expansion pattern already used in other Recall scripts.
- The real blocker did not change: local key-rotation evidence is still stale/missing, and dry-run plus backup proof are stale behind that gate.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

**Created:**
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_CORE_APPLY_KEY_ROTATION_EVIDENCE_GATE_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_01-18-04_IST.md` - no-secret milestone report for the core apply key-evidence gate.

- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_CAPPED_APPLY_APPROVAL_PACKET_2026-06-24_19-28-07_IST.md` - lower-level debug apply command now includes key-evidence flags.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SNAPSHOT_IMPORT_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK_2026-06-24_10-47-45_IST.md` - production direct apply example now includes key-evidence flags.

## 2026-07-10 17:24 - F08 attached manual notes kickoff and isolated baseline

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** autonomous end-to-end request to design, implement, validate, document, and release per-item private manual notes with rich Markdown editing, autosave, search, and AI/graph participation

### Planned since last entry

Start from current `origin/main`, preserve unrelated work, create the requested isolated `Phase3` worktree, inspect the existing F08 evidence and live code paths, convene the product/architecture/UX council, produce v1 artifacts, adversarially review them into v2, implement with tests, update the public wiki, and release through the repository's guarded production path.

### Done

- Confirmed `/Users/arun.prakash/Documents/arunvault/arun-cursor/Initiatives/Arun_AI_Projects/ai-brain/Phase3` was an empty folder inside an unrelated modified parent repository, not an AI Brain checkout.
- Located the clean canonical clone at `ai-brain-git-main`, verified its remote is `https://github.com/arunpr614/ai-brain.git`, fetched `origin/main`, and proved local `main` equals `origin/main` at `2b4db95`.
- Created clean worktree `Phase3` on new branch `codex/manual-content-notes` from `main`. Existing user changes and existing worktrees were not touched.
- Read `AGENTS.md`; no Android artifact is being built yet, so the APK versioning rule is not active at this milestone.
- Read the supplied F08 PRD V2 and inspected its reference screenshot. The source is useful for editor-state and autosave hazards, but describes standalone note creation rather than an attached note on every existing library item.
- Mapped the current item-detail route, SQLite schema/migrations, FTS5 search, chunk/vector embedding, semantic retrieval, related-item calculation, design tokens, tests, and guarded Hetzner deployment script.
- Confirmed the product gap: standalone note items exist, but attached per-item user notes do not. Reusing `items.body` would merge source material with private interpretation and violate the requested separation.
- Started three isolated council workstreams: product/project-management council with Growth, Platform/Data, and Power User lenses; technical architecture; and UX/UI design plus prototype. Codex remains coordinator and integration owner. QA/reviewer follows after v1.
- Loaded the Codex running-log, adversarial-review, clean-worktree, and Product Design workflows. Product Design saved context was absent, so the existing repo, design system, and F08 screenshot are the visual baseline.

### Cross-lane notes

- Council agents may write only under `docs/feature-council/F08-manual-content-notes/` and must not edit code, root trackers, or this log.
- Codex owns shared integration, v1/v2 synthesis, production implementation, root documentation, git history, and release.

### Learned

- The requested `Phase3` path resolves through Google Drive but is a valid git worktree location.
- AI Brain is SQLite/local-first, not Supabase; the design must use additive SQLite migrations and the existing local queue/search systems.
- Search currently indexes only item title/body. Semantic retrieval and related items use `chunks`/`chunks_vec`, and `embedItem()` is intentionally no-op once chunks exist. Attached-note edits therefore need both an independent textual index and an explicit safe re-index path.
- Production deployment is guarded by typecheck, lint, tests, environment checks, provider checks, a standalone build, artifact-safety checks, authenticated health checks, and service restart through `scripts/deploy.sh`.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed this milestone.

### Documents created or updated this period

**Updated:**
- `RUNNING_LOG.md` - appended this kickoff and baseline entry.

### Current remaining to-do

1. Receive and reconcile council inputs; create PRD, UX, and technical-plan v1 plus decision tracker.
2. Run evidence-based adversarial review of the full v1 package and resolve all P0/P1/P2 inputs into v2.
3. Implement attached-note storage, optimistic concurrency, autosave/manual save, Markdown editor/preview, search, and semantic/related integration with migrations and tests.
4. Run local functional, accessibility, responsive, security, data-integrity, and release gates; validate design against the reference and accepted v2 package.
5. Update canonical/public wiki documentation, commit/push/PR/merge, deploy to production, and run production smokes with rollback evidence.

### Open questions / decisions needed
