## 2026-07-10 17:47 - F08 three-lens council, formal v1 package, and adversarial gate

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** completion of the Product/AI/PM, UX, and Technical council milestone and the required adversarial review of every major v1 artifact

### Planned since last entry

Complete the three council lanes, reconcile them into formal PRD/UX/technical v1 artifacts plus a tracker/decision log, validate the high-fidelity responsive prototype, run an independent QA review and the adversarial-review skill, then revise every accepted finding into v2 before implementation.

### Done

- Completed a Product/AI/PM council with Growth/Engagement, Platform/Data, and Power User/Workflow lenses. The 3–0 decision is a conditional GO for one attached note per existing item and a no-go on implementing the supplied standalone-note PRD unchanged.
- Completed technical architecture covering separate SQLite storage, canonical Markdown, optimistic/idempotent save, bounded recovery, IndexedDB local durability, note FTS, source-aware chunks, asynchronous indexing, Ask/Related/future-graph provenance, security/privacy, migration, testing, rollout, and rollback.
- Completed UX direction aligned to candidate production source `4d97c45`, including the current AI Memory shell, two-column item view, all five existing mobile item tabs plus Notes, mobile bottom navigation, formatting, truthful save/offline/conflict states, and accessibility behavior.
- Built and visually tested a self-contained high-fidelity HTML prototype at desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844. Production build, Write-Markdown-Write interaction, manual Save, offline return, conflict review/Keep mine, six mobile tabs, bottom nav, no-overflow, and zero browser console errors passed. Prototype dependency caches were removed.
- Created formal PRD v1, UX/UI v1, Technical Plan v1, artifact README, project tracker, and decision log under `docs/feature-council/F08-manual-content-notes/`.
- Ran the adversarial-review skill and wrote a timestamped evidence-first report. Verdict: no-go for implementation from the stale-main branch and no-go for production until three P0s are closed.
- Started the independent QA/reviewer pass over the entire v1 package; its report will be incorporated with the adversarial findings into v2.
- Performed a read-only production/source audit: production has 22 migrations through `020_recall_sync`, 122 items, 0 relational chunks, and 44 vec0 rows. `origin/main` is behind the deployed Recall line, and `rsync --delete` makes a stale-main release destructive.

### Council and review decisions

1. The manual note is one separate attached document per item; it never overwrites original/AI fields and never creates a second card/node.
2. Exact search is synchronous with a successful server save; semantic/Ask/Related convergence is asynchronous and source-labeled.
3. Manual Save and autosave share a no-loss contract with local durability, versions, idempotency, and explicit conflicts.
4. Private means authenticated/non-shared by default, with honest browser/server/backup/provider disclosure; it does not claim end-to-end encryption.
5. Notes are excluded from share/export by default; explicit inclusion must show provenance/privacy.
6. Production migrations begin at 021 only after the deployed Recall history is integrated.

### Adversarial P0 findings

1. A single IndexedDB draft keyed only by `item_id` can let one tab overwrite another tab's only crash-recovery copy. V2 must key/journal dirty drafts per editor instance and prove both survive a forced two-tab crash.
2. Production's 0 chunks / 44 vec rows can collide with the first bridge rowid or be deleted unsafely. A manifest-based report-only audit, backup, approved repair/reservation, and parity proof must precede note indexing.
3. The current feature branch does not yet contain the source/migrations/scheduler runtime that production uses. The candidate `4d97c45` source line must be positively inventoried/integrated, baseline-tested, and dry-run compared before any implementation deployment.

### Other required v2 revisions

- Resolve the provenance contradiction: current item embeddings concatenate original body and AI summary, while v1 claims Original/AI/My-note separation.
- Apply `private, no-store` to every note-bearing surface, including search snippets, Ask/citations, conflicts, revisions, and explicit exports.
- Select the editor only after a fixed React/Next/Capacitor/IME/a11y/round-trip/bundle spike; revise the UX deliberately if the visual editor is rejected.
- Add provider-specific first-use acknowledgement before remote transmission, supervised single-worker lifecycle, durable delete/AI-opt-out tombstones, evaluated Related weighting, a 100 KiB default cap unless measured otherwise, and a minimal revision recovery surface.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed or mutated in production. All production work in this milestone was read-only.
---
- **Current phase / version:** Recall daily sync is live-validated; first capped apply has status, key-evidence, core apply key-evidence, prepare-wrapper, refresh, acknowledgement, approval, and ready-state guidance gates. It remains blocked until actual key rotation evidence and fresh proof pass.
- **Active branch(es):** `codex/ai-brain-ux-v2-execution`.
- **Working tree:** Dirty with Recall scripts/docs plus pre-existing unrelated tracked and untracked work; private proof/evidence files remain ignored/untracked; no stage/commit/push performed.
- **Deployed/runtime state:** No Recall production apply, real proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, or checkpoint advancement performed.
- **Next milestone:** Rotate the Recall API key, run the post-rotation prepare wrapper to record/pass private key evidence and refresh stale proof, then run guarded first capped apply only with exact approval and acknowledgement.

## 2026-07-10 18:08 - F08 QA-reviewed v2 contract and attested production baseline

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** completion of independent QA, resolution of all specification P0/P1 findings into v2, and live hash attestation of the production source snapshot

### Done

- Received independent QA review with seven P0 findings, prioritized P1-P3 findings, requirements traceability, a 36-test matrix, and nine release gates. Verdict was v1/production no-go and immediate v2 remediation go.
- Positively distinguished the consolidated production content baseline. Live `/opt/brain/public/manifest.webmanifest` and `offline.html` hashes exactly match commit `8178117` and differ from `4d97c45`; live schema includes 018/019/020, while 4d lacks 018/019. Common Recall service/timer/scripts/logo/service-worker also hash-match. Recorded a source attestation report.
- Finalized PRD v2, UX/UI v2, and Technical Plan v2 and wrote a review-disposition matrix. The v2 package resolves every specification P0 and makes every remaining risk an executable no-go gate.

### Final v2 changes

- Replaced one item-keyed local draft with per-editor-instance monotonic IndexedDB journals so two dirty tabs survive a crash independently.
- Added an item-scoped content-free note epoch/tombstone retained until parent deletion; delayed null-base/offline drafts cannot resurrect a deleted note, and explicit recreation starts a new epoch.
- Defined immutable payload-specific save envelopes: exact retry reuses the mutation ID; any changed queued content gets a new ID; edit/manual-save during flight never overlaps or mutates the request.
- Made AI opt-out a versioned synchronous eligibility boundary in retrieval, Ask, Related, and worker pre-call/pre-commit checks. Vector deletion is cleanup, not the privacy boundary.
- Finalized remote-provider behavior: exact search is independent; any remote note-consuming provider requires provider-named one-time acknowledgement before note transmission; per-note opt-out remains.
- Finalized v1 editor as a native controlled Markdown textarea with selection-aware toolbar and safe Preview, using a defined Markdown/GFM subset and 100 KiB UTF-8 cap. WYSIWYG is deferred.
- Expanded semantic provenance to `legacy_item_context`, `original_content`, `ai_summary`, and `manual_note`; legacy title/summary/body chunks are labeled Saved item context, never Original.
- Defined streamed/persisted citation source/version behavior and truthful degradation. Note Delete also accounts for item-chat turns derived from the deleted note.
- Defined a supervised leased concurrency-one worker, explicit vec0 cleanup, durable/safe rowid allocation, and report-only production vector audit/repair gates.
- Scoped shipped connection behavior honestly to evaluated note-aware Related plus a future graph event contract; no nonexistent persisted graph is claimed.
- Finalized privacy/export/revision/cache behavior and distinguished current-client cleanup, offline-client tombstone reconciliation/copy-only drafts, and backup retention.
- Added independent default-off UI, write, and worker flags while explicitly recognizing that migration preflight must happen before startup because flags do not stop auto-migrations.

### Artifacts

- `prd/PRD_F08_MANUAL_CONTENT_NOTES_v2.md`
- `ux/UX_F08_MANUAL_CONTENT_NOTES_v2.md`
- `technical/TECH_F08_MANUAL_CONTENT_NOTES_v2.md`
- `reviews/QA_REVIEW_v1.md`
- `reviews/V1_REVIEW_DISPOSITION.md`
- `validation/PRODUCTION_SOURCE_BASELINE_ATTESTATION_2026-07-10.md`
- Updated tracker, decision log, README, and this running log.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed or mutated. Production evidence remained read-only.

## Entry #167 - 2026-06-25 02:06 IST - Live auth probe now reports key-rotation context without unlocking writes

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal and the user-approved objective to fix the confusion where "the live call still did not run because the local private gates stopped first."

### Planned since last entry

Entry #166 made `npm run recall:first-apply:status` show the optional no-write live auth probe separately from first-write safety. The next no-write hardening target was the optional probe itself: if an operator runs only `npm run recall:live-auth-probe`, its output should show key-rotation context and still say it does not authorize proof refresh or apply.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/run-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs`.
  - Added `firstWriteSafety` to success and failure JSON after env-file loading.
  - Added default key-rotation checkpoint `2026-06-24T15:54:17.000Z` with override flag `--key-rotated-after`.
  - The probe now reports `envFileMtimeIso`, `envFileMtimeAfterCheckpoint`, `keyRotationEvidenceGateRun: false`, `proofRefreshAllowedByThisProbe: false`, and `applyAllowedByThisProbe: false`.
  - A stale private env-file mtime now produces a diagnostic note that read-only connectivity may still be tested but first-write preparation remains blocked until key-rotation evidence passes.
  - A fresh private env-file mtime still does not imply key evidence passed; the dedicated gate/status commands remain required.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs`.
  - Added local private env-file fixtures under ignored `data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-auth-probe-smoke-*`.
  - Smoke now covers no-env, stale-env, and fresh-env key-rotation context with fake local `sk_test...` keys and a local mock server only.
  - Smoke proves stale/fresh env-file context does not permit proof refresh or apply and does not leak API keys or private card fields.
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_LIVE_AUTH_PROBE_KEY_ROTATION_CONTEXT_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-03-04_IST.md`.
- Updated current evidence docs and gates:
  - `scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs` now scans 38 curated Recall docs;
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now checks the new report, `RDS-026b18a`, and the new tracker/audit snippets;

  - project tracker now has the report row, task `RDS-026b18a`, and evidence index entry #87;

- Fixed one approval-packet checker mismatch after the first run failed because the new report did not include the exact phrase `No live Recall API call was made`; appended that exact sentence to the report and reran the gate successfully.

## Entry #170 - 2026-06-25 02:24 IST - Post-rotation prepare wrapper now proves tainted private evidence is non-recordable

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after Entry #169 hardened private key-rotation evidence against secret-shaped JSON content.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #169 made `scripts/check-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs` reject secret-shaped values inside the private key-rotation evidence JSON. This session picked up the next no-write gap: prove that the post-rotation prepare wrapper does not bypass that gate and does not overwrite a tainted private evidence file while attempting to prepare first-write proof refresh/apply. The broader goal remains to unblock the live Recall first-write path safely; production write work is still gated on real key rotation evidence.

  - Added a tainted private evidence fixture with a fake secret-shaped value.

  - Verified the wrapper fails with `non_recordable_key_evidence_failure` and the specific secret-shape rule before it can record fresh evidence.
  - Verified the fake secret-shaped value is not echoed in stderr.
  - Verified the existing tainted evidence file remains untouched after failure, so the wrapper does not hide or overwrite unsafe evidence.
- Confirmed the implementation behavior already fails closed because `scripts/prepare-recall-first-apply-after-rotation.mjs` only treats a narrow set of key-evidence failures as recordable; the new secret-shape rules are intentionally non-recordable.

  - production runbook now tells operators to remove tainted private evidence and record again after verifying the rotated key is stored only in the ignored private env file;
  - project tracker now has the report row, task `RDS-026b18d`, and evidence index entry #90;

## Entry #171 - 2026-06-25 02:37 IST - First-apply status now exposes a status-preserving live diagnostic wrapper

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after Entry #170 proved the post-rotation prepare wrapper refuses tainted private evidence.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #170 left the true first-write path correctly blocked by stale/missing key-rotation evidence and stale proof. This session targeted the remaining operator ambiguity behind the user's complaint that the live call had not run because local private gates stopped first: keep first-write gates intact, but add a one-command, status-preserving path that can run only the read-only live connectivity diagnostic when explicitly confirmed.

### Done

- Added `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Runs `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs` first.
  - Requires explicit live confirmation through `--confirm-live-api` or `BRAIN_RECALL_CONFIRM_LIVE_API=1`.
  - Proceeds only when status exposes a live-read diagnostic path.
  - Runs exactly one read-only Recall `/cards` auth probe through the existing `scripts/run-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs`.
  - Emits a combined no-secret summary of the pre-probe status, live-read diagnostic result, and first-write safety state.
  - Does not create or refresh proof, read/write the AI Brain database, apply, deploy, enable a scheduler, or advance a checkpoint.
- Added `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Uses a local mock Recall server and private ignored fixture env/manifest.
  - Proves the wrapper refuses without explicit live confirmation.
  - Proves the wrapper preserves `blocked_key_rotation_evidence` while making exactly one read-only `/cards` request.
  - Proves output includes only status/count metadata and does not print env contents, keys, private card IDs, titles, source URLs, or raw response bodies.
- Updated `package.json` with:
  - `recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic`
  - `smoke:recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic`
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Adds `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.optionalNoWriteWrapperCommand`.
  - Keeps the lower-level `optionalNoWriteCommand`.
  - Tells operators to prefer the wrapper when they want the status-preserving diagnostic route.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-status.mjs` so live-ready/stale-key status must expose the wrapper command while keeping proof refresh/apply blocked.
- Created `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md`.
- Updated current docs and gates:
  - `scripts/check-recall-public-docs-privacy.mjs` now scans 42 curated Recall docs;
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now checks 30 docs and 42 required package scripts;
  - production runbook now names `optionalNoWriteWrapperCommand` and explains that both live diagnostic commands are read-only and do not satisfy first-write gates;
  - project tracker now has the report row, `RDS-026b18e`, exact wrapper evidence snippet, and evidence index entry #91;
  - current-state audit now records the new report, the 42-file public-doc scan, and the new completion matrix row.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #171 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- Entry #164 remains misplaced earlier in the file; Entry #165 documents that correction. Do not move or edit prior entries.
- This change adds an optional read-only diagnostic wrapper only; it intentionally does not weaken key-rotation evidence, proof freshness, approval, apply, deploy, scheduler, or checkpoint gates.
- No real Recall live API call, production dry-run, proof refresh, apply, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or pull request occurred.

### Learned

- The status command already separated live-read connectivity from first-write safety, but the safer operator experience is to expose a wrapper that preserves status first and then runs only the read-only probe.
- The smoke harness initially timed out because it used a blocking child process while the local mock HTTP server lived in the same Node event loop. Switching the smoke runner to async `spawn` allowed the mock server to respond.
- The real status remains `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`: the private env file mtime is still older than the rotation checkpoint and the private evidence JSON is absent.
- The real status now shows `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.optionalNoWriteWrapperCommand` as `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic -- --env-file data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env --confirm-live-api`.

## Entry #175 - 2026-06-25 02:58 IST - First-apply live diagnostic can now ignore stale env files for the read-only probe

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after Entry #174 restored the current EOF handoff.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #174 left the live-read diagnostic discoverable, but still documented the practical safety blocker: the persisted private env file is stale after the chat-exposed key, so a real live call should not rely on that file. This session targeted that remaining local-gate problem without weakening write gates: let the status-preserving live diagnostic use a terminal-only ephemeral key for the actual read-only probe while first-apply status remains blocked for writes.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Added `--probe-no-env-file` to force the lower-level read-only probe to ignore Recall env files.
  - Added `--probe-api-key-env <env-name>` so the probe can read from a named process environment variable such as `RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY`.
  - Added `probeCredential` metadata to success and failure output; it reports env variable name, env-file mode, path/null, presence boolean, and `secretPrinted: false`, never the key value.
  - Added an early `missing_ephemeral_probe_api_key` failure before any live request when `--probe-no-env-file` is requested but the named variable is absent.
  - Kept the first-apply status helper as the first step and kept first-write `blocked_key_rotation_evidence` semantics unchanged.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Existing smoke still proves the wrapper refuses without `--confirm-live-api`, preserves `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`, sends exactly one read-only `/cards` request, prints only status/count metadata, and does not unlock proof refresh or apply.
  - Added negative coverage proving env-file-disabled probing refuses before the probe when `RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY` is absent.
  - Added positive coverage proving env-file-disabled probing sends the Authorization header from `RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY`, while lower-level probe output reports `envFile.loaded === false`.
- Updated docs and consistency gates:
  - wrapper execution report now documents `--probe-no-env-file --probe-api-key-env` and the private-terminal command pattern;
  - production runbook now gives the exact private-terminal flow: `read -rsp`, run `RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY="$RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY" npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic -- --probe-no-env-file --probe-api-key-env RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY --confirm-live-api`, then `unset RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY`;
  - tracker and audit now record the env-file-disabled read-only diagnostic path;
  - approval packet checker now requires the new snippets in the runbook, audit, tracker, and wrapper report.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #175 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- Entry #173 remains out of order around line 9237; Entry #174 documents that correction. Do not move or edit either entry.
- This change affects only the read-only diagnostic path. It does not create key-rotation evidence, refresh proof, apply Recall data, deploy, enable a scheduler, or advance checkpoints.
- No real Recall live API call was run in this session because no fresh rotated key was provided outside chat for the ephemeral path.

### Learned

- The lower-level `scripts/run-recall-live-auth-probe.mjs` already supported `--no-env-file`; the gap was that the first-apply status-preserving wrapper did not expose that capability.
- The first-apply wrapper can now separate three things cleanly:
  - status uses the normal first-apply gates and still reports write blockers;
  - probe credential source can be a terminal-only env variable;
  - proof refresh/apply/deploy/scheduler remain impossible from the diagnostic command.
- The current real status remains `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`: env file mtime is still before `2026-06-24T15:54:17.000Z`, private key-rotation evidence JSON is absent, and dry-run/backup proof are stale.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

### Documents created or updated this period

**Created:** none.

**Updated:**
- `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs` - adds env-file-disabled ephemeral credential mode for the read-only probe.
- `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs` - adds missing-ephemeral-key negative coverage and positive `RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY` probe coverage.
- `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` - requires the new ephemeral diagnostic snippets in current docs.

## Entry #176 - 2026-06-25 03:06 IST - Prompt wrapper added for the first-apply read-only live diagnostic

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after Entry #175 added env-file-disabled probe credentials.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #175 made the read-only diagnostic technically able to ignore the stale persisted env file, but the operator still had to type a multi-command shell sequence with `RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY`. This session removed that avoidable manual sharp edge by adding a human-facing prompt command that collects a key locally, hides it in an interactive terminal, and still forces the child diagnostic to use `--probe-no-env-file`.

### Done

- Added `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs`.
  - Requires `--confirm-live-api` or `BRAIN_RECALL_CONFIRM_LIVE_API=1` before prompting.
  - Prompts locally for the Recall API key with hidden TTY input.
  - Supports `--read-key-from-stdin` only for non-interactive smoke coverage.
  - Passes the key only to the child process as `RECALL_PROMPT_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_API_KEY`.
  - Forces `--probe-no-env-file --probe-api-key-env RECALL_PROMPT_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_API_KEY` for the child diagnostic.
  - Rejects caller-provided `--probe-no-env-file`, `--probe-api-key-env`, and `--probe-env-file` because the prompt wrapper controls the probe credential mode.
  - Does not write the key to disk, refresh proof, apply, deploy, enable a scheduler, or advance checkpoints.
- Updated `package.json` with `recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt`.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Added prompt-wrapper coverage by piping a fake key through stdin to the prompt wrapper.
  - Proves the child still preserves `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`, disables env-file loading, reports the internal prompt env name only, and sends exactly one read-only `/cards` request to the local mock server.
- Updated current docs/checks:
  - wrapper report now names the prompt command as the preferred human route and keeps the non-prompt `RECALL_EPHEMERAL_API_KEY` command as the lower-level equivalent;
  - production runbook now says to prefer `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --confirm-live-api` when the persisted env file is stale or intentionally not trusted for read-only diagnostics;
  - project tracker and completion audit now record the prompt wrapper and `RECALL_PROMPT_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_API_KEY` child-process variable;
  - approval packet checker now requires the prompt script, package script, and prompt-wrapper snippets.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #176 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- Entry #173 remains out of order around line 9237; Entry #174 documents that correction. Do not move or edit either entry.
- This is still read-only diagnostic work. It does not satisfy key-rotation evidence, proof freshness, first-write approval, apply, deploy, scheduler, or checkpoint gates.
- No real Recall live API call was run in this session because no fresh rotated key was provided outside chat.

### Learned

- The safest human UX is now a single prompt command rather than asking the operator to manually export/unset a key variable.
- The prompt wrapper is intentionally a thin layer over the existing first-apply diagnostic so the status helper still runs first and the lower-level probe still emits the same no-secret summary.
- Current first-apply status now reports approval packet green with 30 checked docs and 43 required package scripts, while write readiness remains blocked by missing/stale key-rotation evidence and stale proof.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

### Documents created or updated this period

**Created:**
- `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs` - local prompt wrapper for the env-file-disabled read-only live diagnostic.

**Updated:**
- `package.json` - adds `recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt`.
- `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs` - adds prompt-wrapper stdin smoke coverage.
- `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` - requires the prompt wrapper script, package script, and doc snippets.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` - documents the prompt wrapper and safety boundary.

## Entry #178 - 2026-06-25 03:22 IST - Node env-file interception fixed for first-apply live diagnostics

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Active goal continuation: fix the remaining issue where the live call did not run because local private gates stopped first.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #177 made `npm run recall:first-apply:status` point directly to the prompt-based read-only live diagnostic. The remaining planned work was to verify whether any local private gate could still stop the prompt/live diagnostic before the actual read-only probe path, especially when `recall.env` is stale, missing, or intentionally not trusted.

### Done

- Found and fixed a concrete runtime root cause: Node 22 has a built-in `--env-file` option. When AI Brain package scripts or child spawns passed the project-level `--env-file` flag without Node's `--` separator, Node could consume that flag before Recall wrapper code ran.
  - If the env file was missing, Node stopped before AI Brain could report a controlled JSON failure.
  - If the env file existed, Node could preload it before wrapper-owned env-file safety or env-file-disabled probe logic.
- Updated operator-facing live/readiness package scripts in `package.json` to use `node -- ...` where `--env-file` is likely to be passed:
  - `recall:live-spikes`
  - `recall:live-auth-probe`
  - `recall:live-gate:status`
  - `recall:live-gate:require-ready`
  - `check:recall-first-apply-readiness`
  - `recall:first-apply:status`
  - `recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic`
  - `recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt`
- Updated nested Node child spawns to use the same separator:
  - `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs` now spawns readiness/key-evidence checks as `node -- <script> ...`.
  - `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs` now spawns status/probe children as `node -- <script> ...`.
  - `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs` now spawns the child diagnostic as `node -- <script> ...`.
  - `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs` now starts the wrapper/prompt children with `node -- <script> ...`.
- Hardened `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Added `localPrivateGateHandling` to the wrapper output.
  - It records whether the status live gate was ready, whether a status optional command was available, whether env-file-disabled probing was requested, whether an ephemeral credential was present, and whether the wrapper intentionally bypassed the local live gate only for the read-only env-file-disabled probe.
  - Reordered guard failures so `--probe-no-env-file` without the named terminal-only key reports `missing_ephemeral_probe_api_key` before blaming status/local gate availability.
- Hardened `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Added a regression case where the prompt wrapper receives a missing local `recall.env` path, keeps env-file loading disabled, reports `localPrivateGateHandling.bypassedLocalLiveGateForReadOnlyProbe: true`, and still makes exactly one read-only `/cards` call against the local smoke server.
  - Strengthened test-secret output scanning to reject any `sk_test_...` value in stdout/stderr.
- Updated docs/checks:
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` now records the Node 22 `--env-file` root cause, the `node --` separator fix, `localPrivateGateHandling`, and the missing-env prompt smoke proof.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` now records the same in the report summary, `RDS-026b18e`, and milestone note 91.
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires the new wrapper-report evidence snippets.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #178 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- Entry #173 remains out of order around line 9237; Entry #174 documents that correction. Do not move or edit either entry.
- This work fixes diagnostic call path ownership and operator command reliability. It still does not satisfy key-rotation evidence, proof freshness, first-write approval, apply, deploy, scheduler, or checkpoint gates.
- No real Recall live API call was run in this session. The only `/cards` calls were local smoke-server requests.

### Learned

- Node 22 can treat `--env-file` as a Node option even when it appears after the script path in the practical command shapes used here. The safe pattern for package scripts that accept an app-level `--env-file` flag is `node -- scripts/name.mjs ...`.
- The prompt wrapper was the right operator path, but it also needed proof that a missing local env file cannot stop the env-file-disabled read-only probe before the terminal-only key path runs.
- The controlled missing-env command now fails inside AI Brain JSON as `missing_ephemeral_probe_api_key` when no terminal-only key is present, instead of failing as `node: ... not found`.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

### Documents created or updated this period

**Created:** none.

**Updated:**
- `package.json` - adds `node --` separators to operator-facing Recall live/readiness scripts that accept app-level `--env-file`.
- `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs` - uses `node --` for child readiness/key-evidence checks.
- `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs` - uses `node --` for child status/probe checks, adds `localPrivateGateHandling`, and reports missing ephemeral keys before local-gate availability.
- `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs` - uses `node --` for the child diagnostic.
- `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs` - covers missing local env-file prompt probing and stronger secret-output assertions.
- `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` - requires the new Node separator/local-gate bypass evidence in the wrapper report.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` - records root cause, fix, and validation evidence.
- `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` - updates the tracker row, RDS-026b18e, and milestone note.
- `RUNNING_LOG.md` - appended Entry #178 at EOF.

git diff --check -- package.json scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md RUNNING_LOG.md

### Current remaining to-do

1. If Arun wants the read-only live Recall call before writing any key to disk, run `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --confirm-live-api` and type a freshly rotated key locally into the hidden prompt. Do not paste the key in chat.

## Entry #177 - 2026-06-25 03:12 IST - First-apply status now points directly to the prompt live diagnostic

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continuing the active Recall daily sync goal after Entry #176 added the prompt wrapper but status still preferred the env-file wrapper.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #176 created `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt`, but `npm run recall:first-apply:status` still surfaced the env-file wrapper as the first optional diagnostic. That preserved an operator trap: the safest human route existed, but the status helper still nudged the operator toward the stale env file. This session made the prompt command the first-class status recommendation while preserving all first-write gates.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Added `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.optionalNoWritePromptCommand`.
  - `optionalDiagnosticCommands[0]` is now `first_apply_live_diagnostic_prompt` with `command: npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --confirm-live-api`, `mode: read_only`, `preferred: true`, and `credentialMode: local_prompt_env_file_disabled`.
  - The env-file wrapper remains present as `optionalDiagnosticCommands[1]` with `preferred: false`.
  - The lower-level auth probe remains present as `optionalDiagnosticCommands[2]` with `preferred: false`.
  - `readOnlyDiagnosticNextAction` now names the prompt command instead of the stale-env wrapper.
  - Safety notes still state the diagnostic does not satisfy key rotation evidence, proof freshness, write approval, apply, deploy, scheduler, or checkpoint gates.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Proves live-ready/stale-key status exposes the prompt command.
  - Proves the prompt command is first in top-level optional diagnostics and carries `local_prompt_env_file_disabled`.
  - Proves the env-file wrapper is still present as the second, non-preferred optional diagnostic.
- Updated current docs/checks:
  - wrapper report now records `optionalNoWritePromptCommand`, `first_apply_live_diagnostic_prompt`, and `credentialMode: local_prompt_env_file_disabled`;
  - production runbook now describes the status output as prompt-first and env-file-wrapper-second;
  - project tracker and completion audit now record prompt-first status behavior;
  - approval packet checker now requires the new status-shape snippets.

### Cross-lane notes

- Entry #177 is now the latest physical append and current pickup point.
- Entry #173 remains out of order around line 9237; Entry #174 documents that correction. Do not move or edit either entry.
- This is still read-only diagnostic discoverability work. It does not satisfy key-rotation evidence, proof freshness, first-write approval, apply, deploy, scheduler, or checkpoint gates.
- No real Recall live API call was run in this session because no fresh rotated key was provided outside chat.

### Learned

- Creating a safe prompt command was not enough; the status helper needed to make it the first recommendation or future operators could still follow the stale-env path.
- The current real status now cleanly separates:
  - prompt live diagnostic: read-only, preferred, env-file disabled;
  - env-file wrapper: read-only, available, not preferred;
  - first-write path: still blocked on key-rotation evidence and stale proof.
- Current first-apply status still reports approval packet green with 30 checked docs and 43 required package scripts.

## Entry #181 - 2026-06-25 03:35 IST - Env-file separator hardening extended across Recall wrappers

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to ensure the live call is not stopped by local private gates or Node option parsing before AI Brain wrapper logic runs.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #180 corrected the running-log tail after Entries #178/#179 landed out of physical order. Entry #178 had fixed the first-apply prompt diagnostic path, but the next audit needed to prove the same Node 22 `--env-file` interception issue could not affect the rest of the Recall live/readiness and first-write wrapper chain.

### Done

- Extended the Node argument-separator fix beyond the prompt diagnostic:
  - `package.json` now uses `node --` for `check:recall-key-rotation-evidence`, `recall:key-rotation-evidence:record`, `recall:first-apply:prepare-after-rotation`, and the Recall smoke scripts that exercise env-file paths.
  - `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs`, `scripts/check-recall-live-gate-status.mjs`, `scripts/prepare-recall-first-apply-after-rotation.mjs`, `scripts/record-recall-key-rotation-evidence.mjs`, and `scripts/run-recall-live-spikes.mjs` now spawn Node child scripts with a script separator.
  - `scripts/run-recall-live-spikes.mjs` preserves Node options by placing `--` after `--import tsx`, not before it.
  - Smoke harnesses that launch env-file-bearing scripts now use the same separator.
- Hardened first-write shell wrappers:
  - `scripts/recall-first-apply-ready-or-refresh.sh`
  - `scripts/recall-first-apply-proof-refresh.sh`
  - `scripts/recall-first-capped-apply.sh`
  - `scripts/recall-scheduled-apply.sh`
  These now call `node -- scripts/...` or `node --import tsx -- scripts/sync-recall.ts ...` where app-level flags may include `--env-file`.
- Improved parent readiness diagnostics:
  - `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs` now preserves child key-rotation evidence findings in `checked[].details.findings`.
  - `npm run smoke:recall-first-apply-readiness` proves stale key evidence still identifies `env_file_not_rotated_after_checkpoint` without printing env contents.
- Updated docs/checks:
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` now documents the broader env-file boundary hardening, `--import tsx` ordering nuance, and package-script scan.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` now records the broader fix in the summary row, RDS-026b18e, and milestone note 91.
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires the new documentation snippets.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #181 as latest physical tail.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain out of physical order before Entry #177; Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit old entries.
- This is still offline/local hardening. It did not run a real Recall live API call, proof refresh, apply, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, or push.

### Learned

- The initial prompt-diagnostic fix was necessary but not sufficient. Any `node scripts/foo.mjs --env-file ...` path can let Node 22 consume the app-level `--env-file` before AI Brain logic runs.
- The safe pattern is:
  - `node -- scripts/foo.mjs --env-file ...` for normal `.mjs` scripts;
  - `node --import tsx -- scripts/sync-recall.ts --env-file ...` when Node options are needed before the script.
- Parent readiness should preserve child gate rules; otherwise operators see a generic gate failure and lose the actionable reason.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

### Documents created or updated this period

**Created:** none.

**Updated:**

- `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-readiness.mjs` - uses separator for child gates and preserves child key-evidence findings.
- `scripts/prepare-recall-first-apply-after-rotation.mjs` - uses separator for child commands.

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work after Entry #181 to make the Node 22 `--env-file` local-gate fix executable and regression-proof.

Entry #181 extended the Node `--env-file` separator hardening across Recall package scripts, child spawns, proof refresh, first capped apply, and scheduled apply. The remaining hardening gap was that part of the proof was still a manual package-script scan in documentation. This session turned that scan into a first-class checker and wired it into the approval packet.

### Done

- Added `scripts/check-recall-node-env-file-separators.mjs`.
  - Checks Recall package scripts that can receive AI Brain app-level `--env-file` arguments and requires a Node script separator.
  - Checks the first-apply/scheduled shell wrappers for `node -- scripts/...` and `node --import tsx -- scripts/sync-recall.ts` patterns.
  - Checks child-spawn call sites in status, readiness, live diagnostic, prompt diagnostic, prepare, key-evidence recorder, live gate status, and live-spike runner files.
  - Emits checked labels for `package env-file scripts use node separator`, `shell wrappers use node separator for env-file gates`, `child spawns use node separator for env-file gates`, and `tsx commands place separator after node options`.
- Added `check:recall-node-env-file-separators` to `package.json` as `node -- scripts/check-recall-node-env-file-separators.mjs`.
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs`.
  - Required package scripts now include `check:recall-node-env-file-separators`; approval packet currently reports 44 required scripts.
  - The first-apply live diagnostic wrapper report now must mention the new checker and the package env-file separator invariant.
- Updated public Recall docs:

- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or real Recall API call was run.

### Learned

- The original failure class needs a durable static guard because the fragile state is distributed: package scripts, shell wrappers, and child spawns all have separate ways to accidentally reintroduce Node's pre-script `--env-file` parsing.
- The approval packet is a good place to require this guard because `npm run recall:first-apply:status` surfaces approval-packet health inside first-write readiness.
- Current real status now reports approval-packet details with `checkedDocs: 30` and `requiredScripts: 44`.

### Deployed / Released

## Entry #183 - 2026-06-25 03:51 IST - Prompt diagnostic rejects probe credential overrides before key entry

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to remove the last operator ambiguity before a real read-only Recall diagnostic can be run with a locally typed rotated key.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #182 added a durable env-file separator regression checker and left the next live-read path as `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --confirm-live-api`. The remaining edge was that the prompt wrapper forced env-file-disabled probing, but caller-supplied probe credential flags in equals form could still be passed through to the child and fail only after the key had been entered.

### Done

- Hardened `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs`.
  - Added a controlled probe-argument denylist for `--probe-no-env-file`, `--probe-api-key-env`, `--probe-env-file`, `--api-key-env`, and `--no-env-file`, including equals-form variants.
  - The prompt wrapper now fails with `controlled_probe_argument` before reading stdin or opening the hidden terminal prompt.
  - Prompt wrapper failure output now redacts secret-shaped values before printing the message.
  - Help text now states that probe credential and env-file flags are controlled by the wrapper and rejected before prompting.
- Expanded `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Added a regression case for `--probe-api-key-env=RECALL_API_KEY`.
  - Proves this contradiction exits before a read-only request, prints no test key, and does not leak private card/title/source details.
  - The smoke's checked list now includes `first-apply live diagnostic prompt wrapper rejects caller-supplied probe credential flags before prompting`.
- Updated public evidence docs and approval checker:

### Learned

- A prompt wrapper should reject contradictory credential-control arguments before prompting, not rely on the child process to fail after a secret has been entered.
- Equals-form flags matter for safety checks. Rejecting only exact two-token flags leaves a avoidable operator footgun.

## Entry #184 - 2026-06-25 03:56 IST - Standalone prompt-guard smoke added to approval path

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to make the prompt diagnostic's pre-key safety check independently runnable before any real Recall credential is entered.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #183 made the prompt wrapper reject caller-supplied probe credential overrides before key entry. The remaining usability gap was that the proof lived inside the larger live-diagnostic smoke. This session added a focused no-live, no-server guard smoke so future agents can verify the prompt pre-key behavior quickly and include it in the approval path.

### Done

- Created `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt-guard.mjs`.
  - Spawns `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs` with `--read-key-from-stdin` and exact/equals-form controlled probe flags.
  - Covers `--probe-api-key-env`, `--probe-env-file`, `--probe-no-env-file`, `--api-key-env`, and `--no-env-file`.
  - Verifies each case exits with `controlled_probe_argument` before a real prompt/live probe path can proceed.
  - Verifies no supplied test key, secret-shaped value, bearer token, or env contents are printed.
- Added `smoke:recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt-guard` to `package.json`.
- Updated `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs`.
  - Required package scripts now include the standalone prompt guard smoke.
  - The first-apply live diagnostic report now must mention `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt-guard.mjs`, `npm run smoke:recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt-guard`, and `prompt guard rejects controlled probe flags before reading stdin`.
  - Approval packet now reports 45 required package scripts.
- Updated public evidence docs:
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md`
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md`
- Confirmed real first-apply status still exposes the preferred read-only prompt command while preserving `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #184 as latest physical tail after verification.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain physically out of order before Entry #177. Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit those entries.
- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or real Recall API call was run.

### Learned

- A tiny no-live guard smoke is better than relying only on the full local-server smoke for pre-key prompt safety. It is faster, has fewer moving parts, and proves the exact behavior an operator cares about before entering a real credential.
- Real first-apply status now reports approval-packet details with `checkedDocs: 30` and `requiredScripts: 45`.

## Entry #185 - 2026-06-25 04:01 IST - First-apply status now surfaces no-live prompt guard

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to make the safe prompt-path self-test visible from the same status output operators use before a live Recall diagnostic.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #184 added a standalone no-live prompt guard smoke, but `npm run recall:first-apply:status` still only surfaced the real prompt/live diagnostic commands. The next usability gap was to expose the no-live self-test directly in first-apply status so operators can verify the prompt wrapper before typing a rotated Recall key.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Adds `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.promptGuardSelfTestCommand`.
  - Adds `optionalDiagnosticCommands[0].id: first_apply_live_diagnostic_prompt_guard`.
  - Marks the guard command as `mode: offline_self_test` and `credentialMode: no_real_key_no_live_api`.
  - Keeps the real prompt command as the preferred read-only diagnostic at `optionalDiagnosticCommands[1]` with `credentialMode: local_prompt_env_file_disabled`.
  - Updates `readOnlyDiagnosticNextAction` to mention both the real prompt command and the no-live prompt guard.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Proves live-ready/key-blocked status exposes the prompt guard self-test command.
  - Proves the prompt guard appears before the real prompt command in `optionalDiagnosticCommands`.
  - Proves the real prompt remains preferred and read-only.
- Updated public evidence docs and approval checker:
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs`
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md`
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md`
- Confirmed real first-apply status still returns `blocked_key_rotation_evidence` with failed checks `key_rotation_evidence`, `dry_run_report_proof`, and `backup_proof`; approval packet is now green with 45 required package scripts.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #185 as latest physical tail after verification.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain physically out of order before Entry #177. Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit those entries.
- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or real Recall API call was run.

### Learned

- Status output is the right operator surface for the no-live guard. Otherwise the safe self-test exists but is too easy to miss at exactly the moment someone is about to type a real key.
- The guard should not replace the real prompt command. It is intentionally an `offline_self_test` that proves prompt safety but does not prove Recall connectivity.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

## Entry #186 - 2026-06-25 04:11 IST - Prompt wrapper now self-checks its no-live guard before key entry

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work after pause to fix the remaining private-gate/live-read ambiguity before a real Recall API diagnostic can be run safely with a locally typed rotated key.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #185 surfaced the no-live prompt guard from `npm run recall:first-apply:status`, but the prompt wrapper itself still relied on the separate guard smoke for proof. The next milestone was to make the wrapper run an internal no-live preflight before any key prompt/stdin read, add direct no-key proof for that behavior, and keep first-write gates blocked.

### Done

- Hardened `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs`.
  - Added an internal no-live prompt guard preflight that checks controlled probe credential/env-file flags and allowed wrapper flags before any Recall key can be requested or read from stdin.
  - Added `--prompt-guard-self-test`, which prints JSON proof that no key was requested/read, no env file was loaded, no Recall API call was made, and no proof refresh/apply/deploy/scheduler/checkpoint occurred.
  - Fails closed with `prompt_guard_preflight_failed` if the guard ever stops recognizing controlled probe flags.
  - Help text now documents the direct self-test and internal pre-key preflight.
- Expanded `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt-guard.mjs`.
  - Runs the direct `--prompt-guard-self-test` path before controlled-argument rejection cases.
  - Asserts the self-test mode, controlled/allowed arg coverage, and no secret-shaped output.
  - Still covers eight exact/equals-form controlled argument cases and proves rejection before stdin is read.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Its checked list now records that the prompt wrapper runs the internal no-live preflight before reading a key.
- Updated first-apply status wording in `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - The status safety note now says `promptGuardSelfTestCommand` verifies the wrapper's internal no-live preflight and unsafe probe override rejection before key entry.
  - The guard command's safety note now states it does not call Recall, load an env file, or read a real key.
- Updated public evidence/docs and gates:
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires the wrapper report/tracker/audit to mention the internal preflight proof.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` records the direct self-test, automatic pre-key preflight, and updated validation.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` updates RDS-026b18e and note 91.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` now reflects `promptGuardSelfTestCommand` and the internal no-live prompt guard preflight.
- Recovered one expected checker miss:
  - First `npm run check:recall-approval-packet` failed because the completion audit did not yet contain `internal no-live prompt guard preflight`.
  - Added that phrase to the audit, reran the checker, and it passed.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #186 as latest physical tail after verification.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain physically out of order before Entry #177. Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit those entries.

## Entry #187 - 2026-06-25 04:18 IST - Status now advertises direct prompt guard self-test before live diagnostic

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to make the read-only Recall diagnostic path easier to execute without local private gates or operator confusion.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #186 added the direct `--prompt-guard-self-test` mode to the prompt wrapper, but `npm run recall:first-apply:status` still advertised the standalone smoke command as `promptGuardSelfTestCommand`. That meant the safest no-key operator command existed but was not the primary status-discovered guard. The next milestone was to make first-apply status point directly to the wrapper self-test, keep the smoke as regression evidence, and fix stale docs that still referenced the older optional-command ordering.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.promptGuardSelfTestCommand` now points to `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --prompt-guard-self-test`.
  - Added `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.promptGuardSmokeCommand: npm run smoke:recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt-guard`.
  - `optionalDiagnosticCommands[0]` now uses the direct no-key self-test as its `command` and keeps the deeper smoke as `regressionCommand`.
  - `readOnlyDiagnosticNextAction` now names the real prompt diagnostic, direct no-live self-test, and regression smoke.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Proves status exposes the direct self-test command.
  - Proves `promptGuardSmokeCommand` remains available.
  - Proves `optionalDiagnosticCommands[0]` uses the direct self-test, keeps the smoke as `regressionCommand`, and still precedes the real read-only prompt command.
- Updated public evidence/docs and gates:
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires `promptGuardSmokeCommand` and `--prompt-guard-self-test` in the relevant docs.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` now distinguishes direct self-test from regression smoke in status discoverability and validation text.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SNAPSHOT_IMPORT_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK_2026-06-24_10-47-45_IST.md` replaced the stale single long paragraph with clearer operator ordering: direct guard at `optionalDiagnosticCommands[0]`, real prompt at `[1]`, env-file wrapper at `[2]`, and lower-level live-auth probe as optional.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` now records `promptGuardSmokeCommand` and the direct `--prompt-guard-self-test` command.

- The production runbook had stale optional-command indexes after the guard was inserted. Rewriting that paragraph into smaller paragraphs made the execution order much clearer.

## Entry #188 - 2026-06-25 04:24 IST - Status exposes prompt diagnostic even when local live gate is not ready

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to prevent local private gates from hiding the read-only Recall prompt diagnostic.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #187 made status advertise the direct no-key prompt guard first, but the real prompt diagnostic itself was still only exposed from status when the local live gate was ready. The next gap was that a missing, stale, or intentionally untrusted local env file could keep status from showing the very prompt command designed to bypass that local env-file gate for a read-only probe.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.optionalNoWritePromptCommand` is now always exposed as `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --confirm-live-api`.
  - Added `promptDiagnosticAvailableWithoutLocalLiveGate: true`.
  - Added `promptDiagnosticBypassesLocalLiveGate`, true when the env-file wrapper/lower-level live probe are not available from the local live gate.
  - Added `promptDiagnosticRequiresLocalKeyEntry: true`.
  - Kept `optionalNoWriteWrapperCommand` and lower-level `optionalNoWriteCommand` hidden until the local live gate is ready.
  - Prompt command safety text now explicitly says it may continue when the local env-file live gate is not ready, but first-write gates remain blocked.
- Expanded `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - The stale-key / live-gate-skipped fixture now proves the prompt diagnostic remains visible.
  - Proves `promptDiagnosticBypassesLocalLiveGate: true` and `promptDiagnosticAvailableWithoutLocalLiveGate: true` in that case.
  - Proves env-file wrapper diagnostics remain null until the local live gate is ready.
  - Live-ready fixture still proves `promptDiagnosticBypassesLocalLiveGate: false` and wrapper/lower-level diagnostics are exposed.
- Updated public evidence/docs and gates:
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires `promptDiagnosticAvailableWithoutLocalLiveGate`, `promptDiagnosticBypassesLocalLiveGate`, and local-live-gate wording in the relevant docs.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` documents that status keeps the prompt command visible even when the local live gate is not ready.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SNAPSHOT_IMPORT_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK_2026-06-24_10-47-45_IST.md` now says status always exposes the prompt diagnostic, while env-file wrapper diagnostics appear only when the local live gate is ready.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` records the new diagnostic flags.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` updates RDS-026b18e and note 91; a first approval-packet run caught that the exact `optionalNoWriteWrapperCommand` phrase had been dropped, and this entry restored it.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #188 as latest physical tail after verification.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain physically out of order before Entry #177. Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit those entries.
- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or real Recall API call was run.
- The Recall API key pasted earlier in chat was not used.

### Learned

- The command intended to bypass a local env-file gate should not be hidden by that same gate in status output.
- The status output now has a cleaner split: prompt diagnostic is always available for a locally typed key with env-file loading disabled; env-file wrapper diagnostics remain gated by local env-file readiness.
- The approval checker again caught useful doc drift: tracker wording lost `optionalNoWriteWrapperCommand` while describing the new hidden-wrapper behavior.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

## Entry #190 - 2026-06-25 04:39 IST - Prompt diagnostic output now carries wrapper guard proof

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to fix the read-only Recall live-call path after local private gates previously stopped it first.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #189 made status JSON clearly mark the real prompt diagnostic as pre-key guarded. The remaining evidence gap was the actual prompt diagnostic output: when the command eventually succeeds with a freshly rotated local key, it previously streamed the child diagnostic JSON directly, so the successful artifact did not itself prove it came through the guarded prompt wrapper.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs`.
  - Successful child diagnostic JSON is now enriched with a top-level `promptWrapper` block.
  - `promptWrapper` records `preKeyGuarded: true`, prompt guard `preflight` counts, `keyEntryMode`, `credentialMode: local_prompt_env_file_disabled`, `childApiKeyEnv: RECALL_PROMPT_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_API_KEY`, `envFileDisabledForProbe: true`, `controlledProbeArgsRejectedBeforeKeyEntry: true`, and `secretPrinted: false`.
  - JSON child errors are also enriched with the same wrapper context when parseable.
  - Non-JSON child output still passes through unchanged.
  - No key is logged; the prompted key is still passed only through the internal child-process environment variable and cleared from the wrapper local variable in `finally`.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Proves ordinary prompt diagnostic output includes `promptWrapper.preKeyGuarded: true`.
  - Proves prompt smoke output includes `keyEntryMode: stdin`, `credentialMode: local_prompt_env_file_disabled`, `childApiKeyEnv: RECALL_PROMPT_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_API_KEY`, `envFileDisabledForProbe: true`, and `controlledProbeArgsRejectedBeforeKeyEntry: true`.
  - Proves prompt smoke output includes internal preflight counts.
  - Proves the missing-env prompt path also carries prompt-wrapper guard proof while bypassing local env-file readiness only for the read-only env-file-disabled probe.
- Updated public docs and approval-packet checks.
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires `promptWrapper.preKeyGuarded`, `promptWrapper.envFileDisabledForProbe`, and `promptWrapper.controlledProbeArgsRejectedBeforeKeyEntry`; the wrapper report and tracker also require `promptWrapper.childApiKeyEnv`.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` now documents the prompt-wrapper proof block.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SNAPSHOT_IMPORT_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK_2026-06-24_10-47-45_IST.md` now tells operators to expect `promptWrapper.*` fields as artifact-level proof.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` and `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` record the same proof fields.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #190 as latest physical tail after verification.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain physically out of order before Entry #177. Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit those entries.
- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or real Recall API call was run.
- The Recall API key pasted earlier in chat was not used.

### Learned

- Status-level guard metadata is useful before execution, but the eventual live-read artifact also needs wrapper provenance. The `promptWrapper` block now lets a future operator or reviewer see that the successful read-only `/cards` probe came through the guarded prompt path.
- Adding wrapper provenance can be done without changing the child diagnostic contract: existing top-level fields remain, and the wrapper only adds one new metadata block when child JSON is parseable.
- The real current first-apply status still has local live gate ready but first-write gates blocked; this change improves evidence quality for a future prompt diagnostic with a freshly rotated key, not first-write readiness.

## Entry #191 - 2026-06-25 04:46 IST - Prompt diagnostic can write sanitized private output

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to make the read-only Recall live call runnable and durable without local private gates stopping first.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #190 added `promptWrapper` guard proof to successful prompt diagnostic JSON. The next practical gap was durable evidence: when Arun eventually types a freshly rotated key locally, the read-only prompt run should be able to save a sanitized private artifact without shell redirection, without leaking the key, and without unlocking any first-write gates.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/run-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic-prompt.mjs`.
  - Added optional `--output-file <path>` support.
  - Output files must stay under `data/private/recall-live-spikes/`; unsafe paths fail as `output_file_not_private` before live confirmation or key entry.
  - The wrapper writes only parseable, already-sanitized enriched child JSON.
  - The output file is created with owner-only mode `0600`.
  - `--output-file` was added to allowed wrapper args for the internal no-live prompt guard self-test.
  - Help text now shows the durable private output example:
    `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --confirm-live-api --output-file data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-diagnostic-report.json`.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-live-diagnostic.mjs`.
  - Proves `--output-file` outside the private Recall evidence path is rejected before probing and before any live-read request is added.
  - Proves valid private output is written as owner-only `0600`.
  - Proves the private output keeps `promptWrapper.preKeyGuarded: true` and env-file-disabled probe metadata.
  - Proves the private output does not print env contents, keys, private card IDs, private titles, private source details, or raw response bodies.
- Updated public docs and approval-packet checks.
  - `scripts/check-recall-approval-packet.mjs` now requires `--output-file`, `data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-diagnostic-report.json`, `output_file_not_private`, and `owner-only mode \`0600\`` in the relevant docs.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_FIRST_APPLY_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_WRAPPER_EXECUTION_REPORT_2026-06-25_02-27-19_IST.md` now documents private-path-only output and owner-only mode.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SNAPSHOT_IMPORT_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK_2026-06-24_10-47-45_IST.md` now tells operators to use `--output-file data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-diagnostic-report.json` for durable sanitized diagnostic evidence.
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` and `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_PROJECT_TRACKER_2026-06-24_09-16-07_IST.md` record the same behavior.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #191 as latest physical tail after verification.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain physically out of order before Entry #177. Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit those entries.
- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or real Recall API call was run.
- The Recall API key pasted earlier in chat was not used.

### Learned

- A prompt live diagnostic should not rely on shell redirection for durable proof. A private-path-only `--output-file` is less error-prone and makes the security boundary explicit.
- The prompt wrapper can reject unsafe output destinations before asking for a key, which keeps a mistaken path from becoming a post-secret failure.
- The prompt guard self-test now reports `allowedArgCases: 7` because `--output-file` is intentionally allowed at the wrapper layer.

### Deployed / Released

Nothing deployed, applied, pushed, tagged, released, scheduled, proof-refreshed, staged, committed, checkpointed, or PR'd this session.

### Documents created or updated this period

**Created:** none.

**Updated:**

## Entry #192 - 2026-06-25 04:56 IST - Status helper recommends private-output live diagnostic

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work after Entry #191 to finish the local-gate fix for the read-only Recall live diagnostic path.

### Planned since last entry

Entry #191 added private-path-only `--output-file` support to the prompt live diagnostic, but the operator-facing status helper still recommended the bare prompt command. The next useful fix was to make `npm run recall:first-apply:status` point future operators to the durable private-output command by default, so the first real read-only live call with a freshly rotated local key can leave sanitized proof without shell redirection.

### Done

- Updated `scripts/check-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Added `DEFAULT_PROMPT_LIVE_DIAGNOSTIC_OUTPUT` as `data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-diagnostic-report.json`.
  - `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.optionalNoWritePromptCommand` now emits `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic:prompt -- --confirm-live-api --output-file data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-diagnostic-report.json`.
  - Added `diagnostics.liveReadConnectivity.optionalNoWritePromptOutputFile`.
  - Added `outputFile` and `outputFileMode: "0600"` to the preferred `optionalDiagnosticCommands[1]` prompt diagnostic metadata.
  - Updated safety text so the status JSON names the private output file as sanitized diagnostic proof only, not as key evidence, proof freshness, approval, apply, deploy, scheduler, or checkpoint progress.
- Updated `scripts/smoke-recall-first-apply-status.mjs`.
  - Proves the stale-key status path exposes the private-output prompt command and output file field.
  - Proves the live-gate-ready/key-blocked path exposes the same private-output command, `outputFile`, and `outputFileMode: "0600"`.
  - Proves `readOnlyDiagnosticNextAction` includes the `--output-file data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-diagnostic-report.json` form.
- Updated the current Recall docs so the execution report, production runbook, completion audit, and project tracker all describe the private-output prompt command as the preferred status-discovered read-only diagnostic.
- Read and verified the tail of Entry #191 before appending this entry. At that point `RUNNING_LOG.md` had 12,489 lines and latest heading `Entry #191`.

### Cross-lane notes

- Treat Entry #192 as latest physical tail after verification.
- Entries #178 and #179 remain physically out of order before Entry #177. Entry #180 documents that correction. Do not move or edit those entries.
- No production apply, proof refresh, deploy, scheduler enablement, checkpoint advancement, stage, commit, push, or real Recall API call was run.
- The Recall API key pasted earlier in chat was not used.
- The touched Recall scripts/docs are untracked in this worktree, so `git diff` has no tracked baseline for content diffs on those files. Use direct validation commands and marker scans as the authoritative local evidence.

### Learned

- The previous fix made durable private output possible, but discoverability still mattered: a future operator following `npm run recall:first-apply:status` would have run the safe prompt diagnostic without the durable artifact unless the status helper itself carried `--output-file`.
- Status output is the safest place to encode the recommended sequence because it is generated after the current local gate state is known and keeps first-write blockers visible.
- The real current status has `localGateReady: true`, but first-write gates still fail on `key_rotation_evidence`, stale `dry_run_report_proof`, and stale `backup_proof`; the preferred prompt command is read-only diagnostic proof only.

### Deployed / Released

## Entry #193 - 2026-06-25 05:02 IST - First-apply read-only live diagnostic actually ran

**Entry author:** AI agent (Codex) · **Triggered by:** Continued active-goal work to fix the issue where the live Recall call had not run because local private gates stopped first.

The remaining concrete issue in the active goal was that the status-preserving first-apply live diagnostic itself still had not been run in this session. Since the local live-read gate was ready, I ran the env-file wrapper's read-only diagnostic path and then checked that first-write gates stayed blocked.

### Done

- Ran the actual status-preserving env-file live diagnostic:
  - `npm run recall:first-apply:live-diagnostic -- --env-file data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env --confirm-live-api`
  - Result: `ok: true`, wrapper mode `first_apply_live_read_diagnostic`.
  - Status before probe remained `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`.
  - The wrapper made one real read-only `GET /cards` probe for the future window `2100-01-01T00:00:00.000Z` to `2100-01-02T00:00:00.000Z`.
  - Recall returned HTTP `200`, `authenticated: true`, `reachable: true`, `durationMs: 729`, `totalCount: 0`, `resultCount: 0`, and `responseHadResultsArray: true`.
  - The private env file was loaded from `data/private/recall-live-spikes/recall.env`, mode `600`, ignored, untracked, with `loadedKeyCount: 2`.
  - `firstWriteSafety.envFileMtimeAfterCheckpoint` was `false`, so this read-only success still does not satisfy post-chat key-rotation evidence.
- Verified no first-write side effects:
  - Post-run status remained `blocked_key_rotation_evidence`.
  - Failed checks after the run are exactly `key_rotation_evidence`, `dry_run_report_proof`, and `backup_proof`.
  - `proofRefreshAllowedNow: false` and `applyAllowedNow: false`.
  - Existing private proof files stayed stale:
    - `data/private/recall-live-spikes/dry-run-report.json` mtime `2026-06-24 21:10:25 IST`, mode `600`.
    - `data/private/recall-live-spikes/backups/recall-first-apply-20260624T134927Z.sqlite` mtime `2026-06-24 21:10:26 IST`, mode `600`.
  - `data/private/recall-live-spikes/key-rotation-evidence.json` is still missing.
  - `data/private/recall-live-spikes/live-diagnostic-report.json` is still missing, expected because this run used the env-file wrapper, not the prompt wrapper with `--output-file`.
- Created a sanitized public execution report:

  - The report records only status/count metadata and no key material, private card IDs, titles, URLs, chunks, or raw response bodies.
- Updated current state docs:
  - `docs/plans/recall-sync/RECALL_DAILY_SYNC_CURRENT_STATE_COMPLETION_AUDIT_2026-06-24_13-14-45_IST.md` now records the actual env-file diagnostic run and updates the status headline.
